title: 2026 – 2027 Private Debt Platform Treasury Graduate Programme
description: We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed. **Application deadline: April 8, 2026** **The team you’ll join** StepStone Private Debt (SPD) is a global private debt investor with $66B TCR as of 31/12/25\. SPD covers the breadth of the private debt market and constructs bespoke portfolios on a primary, secondary and co\-investment basis. SPD focuses on two areas of the market: performing and non\-performing debt across the corporate, real estate, and infrastructure sectors. SPD is a subsidiary of the StepStone Group, a global private markets firm specializing in Private Equity, Infrastructure, Private Credit and Real Estate. **About The Role** StepStone Group is currently seeking candidates with excellent quantitative, qualitative, and interpersonal skills for a long\-term (6\-12 months) graduate programme within our Private Debt team. The successful candidate will be based in Dublin and will work in a team environment with other StepStone team members to manage all aspects of StepStone’s private debt platform such as capital deployment management, cash management and transaction allocation process across client portfolios. StepStone prides itself on its collegiate atmosphere, encouraging team\-building initiatives and a collaborative approach to accomplishing projects. **What You’ll Do** The successful candidate will have exposure to all levels of the Firm on a daily basis, as well as interacting both with the manager selection and the portfolio management team. The Intern will adhere to the highest ethical standards, and demonstrate maturity, reliability and attentiveness to detail.The Internship opportunity would be suitable for an individual that has recently completed an Undergraduate or Master’s degree. The position is an ideal opportunity to develop valuable learning experiences working across the Private Debt industry. **Key Responsibilities** * Help investment and treasury professionals across all functions of the management of STEP’s platform * Assist Treasury with allocation and settlement of private corporate loans * Monitor cash across multiple private debt investment funds * Assist in managing funding projections provided by managers to incorporate it into the cash planning as well as in the management of credit and leverage facilities * Assist with other projects and ad\-hoc analyses for the Private Debt team * Efficiently communicate views verbally and in written materials **What We’re Looking For** * Must be able to work legally in Ireland without requiring visa sponsorship now or in the future * Graduate from university spring 2026 * BA/BS/MA/MS degree in Finance, Accounting, or other equivalent relevant degree * Demonstrated track record of academic success * Strong written and verbal communication skills and demonstrable analytical capabilities * Proficiency in Microsoft Office Applications (Excel, Word \& PowerPoint) * Ability to multi\-task and prioritize workflow in fast\-paced, detail\-oriented environment * Follow\-up skills and acute attention to accuracy and detail are essential * Team player with strong work ethic **Why join us?** At StepStone, we foster a supportive and inclusive team culture where collaboration and learning are the core of what we do. Our collegial atmosphere encourages teamwork, mentorship, and professional development. This summer internship is a great opportunity to gain practical experience and develop critical skills that will help you launch a successful career in private debt. *At StepStone, we believe that our people are our most important asset and crucial to our success. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer that strives to create an environment that empowers our employees and allows them to be heard, regardless of title or tenure. Our organizational community features multiple Employment Resource Groups as well as mentorship programs to enhance the employee experience for all.* *As an Equal Opportunity Employer, StepStone does not discriminate on the basis of race, creed, color, religion, sex, national origin, citizenship status, age, disability, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, genetic information or any other characteristic protected by law.* *Candidates must be at least 18 years old to apply.* Developing People at StepStone

API integration, REST, GraphQL, and backend service orchestration. * Demonstrated experience in prompt engineering and experimentation with multiple AI models. * Familiarity with Agile methodologies, Scrum practices, and Application Lifecycle Management in a .NET environment. * Experience in automated testing, including unit, integration, and end\-to\-end testing using modern frameworks. * title: Account Executive, EMEA - PerfectScale by DoiT
description: **Location** We are looking to hire an Account Executive.This is a fully remote opportunity, based in the UK or Ireland, with travel as needed. **Who We Are** DoiT is a global technology company that works with cloud\-driven organizations to leverage the cloud to drive business growth and innovation. We combine data, technology, and human expertise to ensure our customers operate in a well\-architected and scalable state \- from planning to production. Delivering DoiT Cloud Intelligence, the only solution that integrates advanced technology with human intelligence, we help our customers solve complex multicloud problems and drive efficiency. With decades of multicloud experience, we have specializations in Kubernetes, GenAI, CloudOps, and more. An award\-winning strategic partner of AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure, we work alongside more than 4,000 customers worldwide. **About DoiT's PerfectScale Platform** DoiT offers PerfectScale, a pioneering Kubernetes optimization and management solution that empowers DevOps, SRE, and Platform Engineering teams to optimize cloud performance while minimizing costs. We combine advanced AI technology with SME\-human expertise to help organizations achieve peak Kubernetes efficiency. The solution delivers a seamless onboarding experience, an intuitive UI, and a powerful autonomous optimization engine that ensures Kubernetes environments run efficiently with minimal human intervention. **The Opportunity** As an Account Executive for the PerfectScale platform, you will apply best\-in\-class pipeline generation, deal management, solution pitching, negotiation, and closing skills to drive growth across enterprise and mid\-market prospects with significant Kubernetes and cloud\-native investments. You will champion our proprietary Kubernetes optimization technology, bringing opportunities through the full sales cycle and helping organizations transform their cloud infrastructure. **Responsibilities** * Deliver against assigned sales and pipeline goals while prioritizing an outstanding customer experience for our prospects * Build out a territory sales plan for PerfectScale's Kubernetes optimization technology * Partner with the Solution Engineering team to drive solution\-focused sales conversations that highlight the value of our Kubernetes optimization platform * Drive business development, forecast accurately, and achieve strategic goals by leading customers through the entire business cycle * Refine the customer sales cycle, focusing on reducing friction to buy and accelerating adoption of PerfectScale's optimization solutions * Contribute to territory strategy in generating and developing business growth opportunities * Explore and understand complex customer requirements on both business and technical levels * Collaborate with marketing team members on pipeline generation campaigns and ensure we capture the true voice of the customer **Qualifications** * BA/BS degree or equivalent practical experience * 5\+ years of quota\-carrying sales experience in B2B SaaS with a consistent record of meeting and exceeding sales quotas * Technical background with experience and understanding of Kubernetes, cloud\-native technologies, and DevOps ecosystems * High degree of self\-discipline, accurate sales forecasting, and CRM management * "Roll up your sleeves" perspective that pervades both sales and business development activities * An entrepreneur at heart who is independent, creative, passionate, and approachable * Self\-organized, goal\-oriented, self\-motivated individual who is confident, thorough, and tenacious * Analytical, data\-driven, detail\-oriented, and able to "zoom" in/out from the big picture to the minutiae * Ability to effectively operate with flexibility in a fast\-paced, constantly evolving team environment * Excellent communication and presentation skills, both written and verbal **Are you a Do'er?** Be your truest self. Work on your terms. Make a difference. We are home to a global team of incredible talent who work remotely and have the flexibility to have a schedule that balances your work and home life. We embrace and support leveling up your skills professionally and personally. What does being a Do'er mean? We're all about being entrepreneurial, pursuing knowledge, and having fun! Click here to learn more about our core values. Sounds too good to be true? Check out our Glassdoor Page. We thought so too, but we're here and happy we hit that 'apply' button. * Unlimited PTO * Flexible Working Options * Health Insurance * Parental Leave * Employee Stock Option Plan * Home Office Allowance * Professional Development Stipend * Peer Recognition Program **Many Do'ers, One Team** DoiT unites as Many Do'ers, One Team, where diversity is more than a goal—it's our strength. We actively cultivate an inclusive, equitable workplace, recognizing that each unique perspective enhances our innovation. By celebrating differences, we create an environment where every individual feels valued, contributing to our collective success. \#LI\-Remote

title: Actuarial Analyst,
description: Optum is a global organization that delivers care, aided by technology to help millions of people live healthier lives. The work you do with our team will directly improve health outcomes by connecting people with the care, pharmacy benefits, data and resources they need to feel their best. Here, you will find a culture guided by diversity and inclusion, talented peers, comprehensive benefits and career development opportunities. Come make an impact on the communities we serve as you help us advance health equity on a global scale. Join us to start **Caring. Connecting. Growing together.** What makes your Actuarial career greater with UnitedHealth Group? Actuaries are the decision\-making engine for our business, that is why we support you from day one. We offer a competitive study program with paid time off to study, expense reimbursement for study materials and pay raises for passing exams. Advancement, access to data, variety of product lines, and rewards for delivering on our mission ensures you will always be challenged to do your best. Our businesses serve the entire spectrum of health care participants: individual consumers and employers, commercial payers and intermediaries, physicians, hospitals, pharmaceutical and medical device manufacturers, and more, providing you with a career that is challenging, exciting, and integral in helping to write the history of our organization. Join at an exciting time as we set up a new team in Ireland supporting UHC new business and renewal pricing. Careers with Optum offer flexible work arrangements and individuals who live and work in the Republic of Ireland will have the opportunity to split their monthly work hours between our Dublin office and telecommuting from a home\-based office in a hybrid work model. **Primary Responsibilities** * Assist in preparing and submitting commercial rate filings for US health insurance business, including Small Group Affordable Care Act (ACA), Large Group, and others * Assist in developing rate recommendations to internal business leaders * Provide input on market strategy, including but not limited to competitive analysis, ad hoc actuarial studies, and other market specific projects * Perform pricing of new products and/or plan designs using the company's proprietary medical and pharmacy benefit pricing tool * Perform rate implementation by updating and maintaining the rating systems for both Small Group and Large Group * Be self\-aware, compassionate, and genuine in service to all team members * Be inclusive, advance diversity; participate in collaborative, engaged, and high\-performing teams You will be rewarded and recognized for your performance in an environment that will challenge you and give you clear direction on what it takes to succeed in your role, as well as providing development for other roles you may be interested in. **Required Qualifications** * Currently in final year of obtaining a bachelor's degree from an accredited college/university or obtained degree within the last 24 months; degree must be obtained prior to start of employment * Proven minimum 2\.2 honor's degree * Proven completion of one or more actuarial exams **Preferred Qualifications** * Proven education in Actuarial Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Accounting, Finance, Economics, or another related technical field from an accredited college/university * Proven actuarial internship or internship in related field * Demonstrated intermediate level of proficiency working with Microsoft Excel, including formulas, calculations, pivot tables, charts, graphs, etc. * Demonstrated basic level of proficiency with MS Word and MS Access **Please note you must currently be eligible to work and remain indefinitely without any restrictions in the country to which you are making an application. Proof will be required to support your application.** *At UnitedHealth Group, our mission is to help people live healthier lives and make the health system work better for everyone. We believe everyone\-of every race, gender, sexuality, age, location and income\-deserves the opportunity to live their healthiest life. Today, however, there are still far too many barriers to good health which are disproportionately experienced by people of color, historically marginalized groups and those with lower incomes. We are committed to mitigating our impact on the environment and enabling and delivering equitable care that addresses health disparities and improves health outcomes \- an enterprise priority reflected in our mission.* *Diversity creates a healthier atmosphere: Optum is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, disability, religion, age, race, and membership of the Traveller community, or any other characteristic protected by law. Optum is a drug\-free workplace. © 2026 Optum Services (Ireland) Limited. All rights reserved.* \#BBMEMEA

title: Applied AI Scientist
description: **Applied AI Scientist** Location: Hybrid / Dublin / Ireland Package: Highly Competitive We are hiring a Senior / **Applied AI Scientist** with deep expertise in **Agentic AI, Generative AI, and Natural Language Understanding (NLU)** to lead high‑impact innovation across our clients Healthcare Information System and MedTech Organisation. This role is suited to a hands‑on research and development leader who thrives on solving complex, real‑world problems and translating advanced AI research into reliable, explainable, and production‑ready healthcare solutions. In this position, the Applied AI Scientist will drive technical breakthroughs, design novel AI architectures, and play a key role in integrating advanced AI capabilities into mission‑critical healthcare products. The role works closely with other AI scientists, engineers, clinicians, and domain experts to ensure solutions are technically robust, ethically sound, and trusted in operational healthcare environments. **Key Responsibilities** **Research \& Innovation** * Lead the design, prototyping, and evaluation of novel AI models for autonomous, agent‑based systems across healthcare workflows. * Advance state‑of‑the‑art Natural Language Understanding for clinical, operational, and administrative healthcare text. * Explore and implement new capabilities in reasoning, decision‑making, and multi‑modal understanding to address real clinical and operational challenges. **Healthcare‑Focused AI Development** * Adapt, fine‑tune, and evaluate large language models to handle healthcare‑specific language, regulatory constraints, and ethical considerations. * Design AI pipelines capable of processing structured and unstructured healthcare data, including FHIR, HL7, clinical notes, and claims data. * Contribute to domain‑specific model architectures that enhance clinical decision support, revenue cycle management, and patient engagement. **Technical Leadership** * Serve as a primary technical authority for Agentic and Generative AI within project teams. * Mentor and support junior AI scientists and engineers through code reviews, research guidance, and technical workshops. * Drive internal knowledge‑sharing around emerging AI trends, research findings, tools, and best practices. **Operational \& Compliance Excellence** * Establish and apply rigorous model evaluation frameworks addressing accuracy, robustness, fairness, and explainability. * Ensure AI systems and data pipelines comply with healthcare privacy and security regulations (e.g. HIPAA, HITRUST). * Partner closely with engineering teams to transition research prototypes into stable, production environments. **Required Qualifications** One of the following: * **Master’s degree** in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, or a related field **and 5\+ years of experience** in AI/ML, with a strong portfolio of deployed models **or** * **PhD** in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, or a related field **and 3\+ years of experience** in AI/ML, with a strong portfolio of deployed models Additional requirements: * Strong experience in Python, R, and modern machine learning libraries * Hands‑on experience with Generative AI, LLMs, and transformer architectures * Experience using AI development frameworks and tools such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, Hugging Face, LangChain, and RAG systems **Preferred Skills** * Experience with multi‑modal AI research (text, images, structured data) * Knowledge graph integration and symbolic reasoning expertise * Demonstrated ability to take AI research from initial concept through to production deployment * Hands‑on experience building autonomous AI agents and reasoning systems * Strong track record applying AI within healthcare or life sciences domains * Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS) and MLOps practices

title: Associate Implementation Specialist (d/f/m) - German Speaking
description: *Personio's intelligent HR platform helps small and medium\-sized organizations unlock the power of people by making complicated, time\-consuming tasks simple and efficient. Our team of 1,500 Personios is building user\-friendly products that delight our 15,000\+ customers and their 1\.5 million employees. Ready to make an impact from day one?* **The Role: How you'll make an impact at Personio** **This position is office\-based (hybrid) in either Munich, Berlin or Dublin** Our Customer Experience team thrives on collaboration, working closely with teams across Personio to ensure that customer voices are at the heart of everything we do. We are dedicated to understanding and advocating for our customers, using data\-driven insights to shape our customer experience strategies and influence what we build. Are you ready to transform how organizations onboard and thrive with Personio? As an **Associate Implementation Specialist (d/f/m) \- German Speaking,** you’ll be the driving force behind our customers’ successful adoption of Personio across the DACH region You’ll empower HR teams to unlock real value, guiding them through every step of their onboarding journey with expertise, empathy, and a dash of Personio flair. **What You’ll Do Customer Impact** * Lead Onboarding: Guide new customers through seamless setup and integration, ensuring Personio becomes an indispensable part of their HR routine. * Product Adoption: Champion product usage, boosting customer retention and lifetime value. * Consultative Guidance: Host regular meetings to understand customer needs, provide tailored advice, and maximize Personio’s impact. * Project Management: Juggle multiple onboarding projects in parallel, keeping timelines and budgets on track while delivering a personalized experience. * Issue Resolution: Triage and troubleshoot customer issues, collaborating with internal teams and escalating bugs as needed. * Relationship Building: Forge strong, trust\-based relationships, handle conflicts with care, and spot opportunities for upselling. * Internal Collaboration: Partner with Sales, Growth, Customer Success, and Product teams to deliver exceptional outcomes and share customer insights. * Knowledge Sharing: Support new joiners, contribute to documentation, and elevate team processes through your expertise. **What You’ll Need To Succeed** * Languages: Fluent in German and English (both mandatory). Additional language is a bonus * Experience: 3\+ years in a customer\-facing SaaS, B2B, or B2C role, with a passion for making customers successful. * Project Management: Proven ability to manage multiple projects and priorities with grace and efficiency. * Tech Savvy: Quick to learn new products and become a Personio expert. * Communication: Outstanding interpersonal skills, able to simplify complex topics and build trust. * Growth Mindset: Adaptable, resilient, and motivated to improve how we deliver value. **Why Personio?** Personio is an equal opportunities employer, committed to building an integrative culture where everyone feels welcomed and supported. We embrace uniqueness and understand that our diverse, values\-driven culture makes us stronger. We are proud to have an inclusive workplace environment that will foster your development no matter your gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, education level, or race. At Personio, we value in\-person collaboration while also offering flexibility. This role is office\-based, with 3 days a week required in your contracted office location. The remaining days can be worked from home or in the office if you prefer. In addition, you’ll have 20 Flex Days per year to work remotely from other locations. Aside from our people, culture, and mission, check out some of the other benefits that make Personio a great place to work: * Receive a competitive reward package – reevaluated each year – that includes salary, benefits, and pre\-IPO equity * Enjoy 28 days of paid vacation, plus an additional day after 2 and 4 years * Make an impact on the environment and society with 1 (fully paid) Impact Day * Receive generous family leave, child support, mental health support, and sabbatical opportunities * We enjoy gathering for meals, cultural initiatives, and events like local Summer Sessions and year\-end celebrations. There's also healthy snacks, drinks, and a weekly catered lunch

title: Associate Legal Counsel - Privacy and Consumer Protection
description: **About Us** Temu is an e\-commerce platform that connects consumers with millions of merchandise partners, manufacturers and brands with the mission of empowering them to live better lives. Temu is committed to offering affordable products to enable consumers and merchandise partners to fulfill their dreams in an inclusive environment. Temu was founded in Boston, Massachusetts in 2022\. The headquarters for our European operations is located in Dublin. Today, we have expanded our footprint to more than 90 countries. **About the Role** Temu is seeking a highly skilled, privacy and consumer protection\-focused Associate Legal Counsel to join our Dublin\-based European legal team. In this role, you will contribute towards ensuring that our platform operates at the highest standards of data security, privacy and consumer protection compliance. In collaboration with your colleagues on the European legal team, you will focus on maintaining our data privacy and security, and consumer protection compliance programmes, and will carry out important research into upcoming regulatory requirements to ensure that we continue to safeguard consumer and regulator trust, while also supporting our ongoing operations and global expansion. This role is based in our Dublin, Ireland office. **Main Responsibilities** * **Strategic Compliance Frameworks** : Support in the management and maintenance of EU data privacy and security, and consumer protection compliance programmes, to ensure alignment with national and EU\-wide regulatory expectations. * **Risk Mitigation \& Audits:** Support internal and external compliance assessments; work closely with cross\-functional teams to proactively identify, track, and remediate data security, privacy, and other regulatory compliance risks. * **Data Governance** : Contribute to the development and maintenance of policies regarding data subject rights under the EU General Data Protection Regulation, the Digital Services Act and other applicable legislation, draft and maintain privacy compliance documents including data protection impact assessments, records of processing activities, legitimate interests assessments, data processing agreements, transfer impact assessments, data breach assessments and notifications, data subject access requests, to ensure our business remains in compliance with applicable regulatory requirements. * **Research** : Ensure that the business identifies early and can prepare for the impact of upcoming changes in legislation by proactively staying on top of developments in applicable law. * **Cross Disciplinary Assistance:** Provide occasional support to our Disputes and EU Competition Law functions, as the need arises, and contribute towards the development of cross\-company awareness of key litigation and EU Competition Law (including DMA) developments. * **Internal Advisory** : Partner with engineering, product, and operations teams to integrate high privacy standards into the development of all EU\-based products and services. * **Third\-Party Oversight** : Manage legal risks associated with vendors and partners, ensuring that data\-sharing ecosystems adhere to the company's rigorous security standards. **Qualifications and Abilities** * Education/Qualifications: Undergraduate degree or above and a qualified solicitor, barrister, or equivalent legal professional licensed to practise law in Ireland, the UK, and/or another EU Member State. * Experience: The ideal candidate will have 1\-3 years of post\-qualification experience, preferably specialising in privacy, data security, consumer protection or both. A background that includes both a premier law firm and an in\-house role in a highly regulated tech or consumer\-facing environment is favourable. * Regulatory Acumen: Experience navigating complex regulatory environments or implementing compliance programmes. * Technical Proficiency: Ability to translate complex legal requirements into actionable guidance for technical teams. * Execution Excellence: Exceptional project management skills, keen attention to detail and the ability to work independently in a dynamic environment with shifting priorities. * Fluency in English (both written and spoken) is a must. Proficiency in any other EU language(s) is a plus. **Compensation \& Benefits** The total salary for this position will be determined based on the candidate’s experience, qualifications, skills and competencies, ensuring a competitive and attractive offer. In addition, this position offers a comprehensive benefits package, including: * Medical and Dental Insurance Coverage * Pension The Company reserves the right to modify or change these benefit programs at any time, with or without notice, consistent with applicable law. We are dedicated to providing a work environment free from discrimination and harassment, where all employees are treated with respect and dignity. Apply today to join Temu and be part of a dynamic and innovative company.

title: Associate Scientist -  Concentrate Development
description: **Job Overview:** **This is a 12\-month fixed term contract.** This role plays a key part in delivering high\-quality beverage concentrates to support global innovation and commercialization. title: BIM Technician
description: **BIM Technician** **Dundalk, Co. Louth, Ireland** **Full\-time / Permanent** **Monday\-Thursday 8am–5pm, Friday 8am–3pm** **Entry level BIM Technicians are welcome** A leading offsite construction and building services provider is looking for a **BIM Technician** to join their growing design and BIM team. The company delivers innovative, prefabricated mechanical and electrical solutions across the UK and Europe, supporting both commercial and public sector projects. The BIM team produces accurate, coordinated, and data\-rich 3D models to support design, offsite manufacture, and onsite installation. By ensuring building services are fully integrated and clash\-free, the team helps streamline construction, reduce errors, and improve collaboration. This entry\-to\-intermediate role offers hands\-on experience in digital design, prefabrication, and BIM workflows. **Key Responsibilities:** * Produce and maintain coordinated 3D Revit MEP models for mechanical and electrical systems * Ensure models are clash\-free and coordinated with architectural and structural elements * Update models to reflect design changes and fabrication revisions * Generate model exports and drawing sets for review and approval * Produce 2D plans, sections, and elevations from 3D models * Support fabrication and installation drawings ensuring accuracy and consistency * Maintain structured file management and version control within the CDE * Collaborate with BIM Coordinators, Engineers, and Project Managers on model coordination and data exchange * Participate in coordination meetings and digital model reviews * Provide up\-to\-date model information to support site and fabrication teams * Engage in ongoing BIM training and development (e.g. Revit and coordination tools) * Contribute to improving BIM workflows, standards, and modelling processes **Candidate Requirements:** **Essential** * 1\+ year experience in BIM, CAD, or MEP design (including internships or placement experience). * Proficient in Autodesk Revit for 3D modelling and coordination. * Familiarity with model review or clash detection software (e.g., Navisworks, Revizto). * Detail\-oriented, with interest in technical design and digital construction. * Strong communication skills and ability to work collaboratively. * Competent in Microsoft Office applications. **Desirable** * Qualification in Building Services Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or Digital Construction. * Experience creating or supporting fabrication\-level models or drawings. * Knowledge of prefabrication or offsite construction workflows. * Understanding of BIM data management and coordination processes.

title: Business Analyst
description: Business Analyst – FinOps Operations Dublin, Ireland \| Hybrid \| Contract via Coyle Consulting Our client, large Tech company in Dublin, is seeking a Business Analyst to join their FinOps team, supporting high‑impact operational programs that sit at the intersection of data analysis, case management, and business performance optimization. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a fast‑paced environment, enjoys solving complex operational problems, and can translate data into actionable insights. As a Business Analyst, you will contribute to analytical workstreams that drive measurable improvements in business performance. You will manage large data sets, support business case development, and produce clear, persuasive insights that guide decision‑making. Within the FinOps team, you will also take ownership of case management and service operations, acting as the primary link between vendor partners and internal sales stakeholders. This role combines analytical depth with operational execution, offering exposure to cross‑functional teams, vendor management, and continuous service improvement initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** • Conduct strategic and data‑driven analysis to identify opportunities that optimize business performance. • Architect metrics, synthesize information, and support problem‑solving across business functions. • Support or lead components of projects involving internal and external integrations between business objectives and operational systems. • Provide analytical support for ad‑hoc data requests from business leaders. • Evaluate new market, product, or cross‑team opportunities to support broader business initiatives. • Independently manage FinOps case queues, ensuring timely and accurate resolution of complex operational issues. • Host office‑hour consultations with internal stakeholders and close service gaps by designing workflows, resources, and processes. • Drive Root Cause Analysis (RCA) and continuous improvement efforts across vendor and operational workflows. **Minimum Qualifications** • Proficiency in statistical analysis, data analysis, and data synthesis. • Strong consultative and storytelling skills with the ability to translate insights into business recommendations. • Experience in project management and business operations. • Technical acumen, including familiarity with experimental design, dashboarding, and data presentation. • Domain‑specific knowledge relevant to business operations, finance, or technology. • Proven experience in case management environments supporting deal making, contracting, or billing systems. • Demonstrated vendor management experience, including workflow design and service creation. • SQL and data visualization skills (or the ability to learn quickly). • Experience with ERP/Billing systems such as Salesforce. • Ability to manage stakeholder expectations while balancing deep analytical work with front‑line operational support.

description: Applicants in San Francisco: Qualified applications with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act. Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: **Dublin, Ireland; Mountain View, CA, USA; New York, NY, USA; San Francisco, CA, USA** . **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, or equivalent practical experience. * 4 years of experience in business intelligence, data analytics, or data science. * Experience with SQL and writing, optimizing, and debugging queries across datasets. * Experience with Python for data manipulation, scripting, or automation. **Preferred qualifications:** * Experience designing and building data warehouses or data mart from scratch (e.g., schema design, ETL orchestration, data modeling). * Experience navigating and fixing legacy data infrastructures or migrating from fragmented systems to a central warehouse. * Experience in business, sales pipeline, or financial reporting. * Experience applying Machine Learning (ML) or AI concepts to BI workflows. * Experience with Salesforce data schemas and CRM analytics. **About The Job** Sellside Monetization plays a key role in Google’s mission, by enabling creators to fund their content by showing advertisements to users. Our group partners with leading web publishers, app developers, and channel partners to grow their businesses using Google’s portfolio of products, including AdSense, AdMob, and Google Ad Manager. We offer a solutions\-based approach across advertising, analytics, and site or app design and pride ourselves on finding new opportunities for our partners to better engage users and advertisers. Over a million publishers and app developers leverage our products and solutions to grow their businesses. We are forming a brand new Central Business Intelligence function to unify our data strategy. We are moving from a fragmented data landscape to a robust, future\-proof infrastructure that will drive business reporting and pipeline visibility and set the foundation for our next generation of AI capabilities. This is not a standard reporting role, it is a builder role. As a Business Intelligence Analyst, you will operate at the intersection of data analysis and data engineering. You will act as a technical anchor, transforming complex data realities into a clean, scalable source of truth that powers decision making for sales, product, and finance leadership. For United States Applicants: The US base salary range for this full\-time position is $117,000\-$167,000 \+ bonus \+ equity \+ benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job\-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process. Please note that the compensation details listed in US role postings reflect the base salary only, and do not include bonus, equity, or benefits. Learn more about benefits at Google . **Responsibilities** * Audit, deconstruct, and rebuild legacy data pipelines to create a single, reliable source of truth for business and pipeline reporting. * Design and implement the data layers required to support future AI and machine learning initiatives for the sales organization. * Write production\-grade, expert\-level SQL and build Python scripts for ETL and automation to bridge the gap between raw data engineering and strategic insights. * Design business intelligence solutions that anticipate future scale, translating complex metrics into formats that enable teams to quickly derive relevant information. * Partner with upstream data providers to establish new source pipelines and influence broader data best practices across the organization. SAP solution certifications preferred * Fluent English required; additional languages a plus **What you’ll build** * Translate customer vision into end‑to‑end business process and data management solutions * Evaluate industry and technology trends to shape innovative process and solution roadmaps * Design, map, and optimize business processes aligned to enterprise IT architectures * Lead solution design across SAP and non‑SAP landscapes (on‑premise, cloud, hybrid) * Drive feasibility studies, solution reviews, and innovation testing initiatives * Represent SAP with customers, delivering high‑quality outcomes on complex, high‑impact engagements * Support presales activities, customer workshops, and executive‑level discussions * Deliver and implement best‑practice business processes across LoBs and global rollouts * Drive change management and support transitions to S/4HANA and cloud solutions * Manage or support complex projects, ensuring milestones, quality, and budgets are met * Contribute to the evolution of SAP’s services portfolio, templates, and delivery assets * Act as (sub‑)project lead or team lead when required, using SAP Activate methodologies * Mentor and coach business process consultants, supporting people development and knowledge sharing * Continuously develop your expertise through certifications, innovation initiatives, and community leadership **Where you belong** At SAP, you will belong to a diverse and inclusive community that values your contributions and supports your growth. title: Change Analyst Graduate
description: **Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres and Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Our Graduate Programme:** We’re seeking the leaders of tomorrow to join us on our Graduate Programme \- those who are willing to share our values, live our culture and deliver beyond expectations. At Mercury we know that to achieve incredible things you must invest in your people. Our dedication to shaping the leaders of tomorrow shines through our highly regarded graduate programme and ensures we stay ahead in a competitive market. From day one you will be on a stellar career trajectory with no limits to what you can achieve, and the programme empowers you to develop the essential skills needed to excel in the fast\-paced construction industry. Since launching in 2009, we have successfully trained 360\+ graduates, many of whom have forged long\-lasting careers with us. Our comprehensive eighteen\-month programme is tailored to meet real\-world demands, featuring courses led by both external and internal subject matter experts in a focused learning environment. Graduates on our programme can anticipate working alongside a diverse and supportive team, accessing state\-of\-the\-art training and masterclasses, and gaining international experience at the leading edge of industry. An important aspect of the development programme is the completion of a group research project. Graduates are split into small groups of four or five and assigned a strategic business project to research and present their findings to Senior Management. All topics are chosen by Senior Management and are relevant to Mercury's business **Role Purpose** An entry\-level opportunity has arisen for a graduate to join our change management team. The Change Analyst role is ideal for an individual seeking experience in transformation, change management and business operations. The Change Analyst provides analytical, coordination and documentation support across Mercury’s change initiatives. This role helps assess impacts, prepare readiness information, produce governance materials, and support the rollout of Mercury’s Change Framework. **Rols \& Responsibilities:** * Business Readiness Support + Support the Change Lead in coordinating readiness activities across teams. + Assist in identifying impacts to people, processes and systems. + Help prepare readiness checklists, stakeholder maps, and cutover support materials. + Gather information from functions to track readiness status, risks and dependencies. * Change Framework \& Documentation + Use and maintain Change Framework templates + Support quality checks on documentation before submission to the CCC. + Help update templates and guidance materials based on lessons learned and feedback. + Maintain SharePoint folders, version control and documentation standards. * Communications \& Stakeholder Engagement + Draft communications, updates and briefing materials for impacted teams. + Support workshop preparation and facilitation (slides, notes, logistics). + Help track stakeholder questions, concerns and follow\-up actions. * Change Control \& Governance Support + Assist with data collection for impact scoring, risk profiles, and dependencies. + Track mitigation plans, decision conditions and follow\-up actions. + Maintain the change decision log and ensure it is kept up to date. * Portfolio \& Reporting Support + Assist PMO with data inputs for portfolio dashboards and reporting. + Track small\-scale changes and help identify collisions or overlaps. + Maintain trackers for readiness, risks, actions and adoption progress. * Continuous Improvement + Capture lessons learned and contribute to process improvements. + Suggest enhancements to templates, tools or ways of working. + Take part in developing Mercury’s maturing Change Function. + Conduct trend analysis on change initiatives approved and rejected **Essential Criteria:** * Graduate degree (Business, HR, Communications, Engineering, Data, or related field). * Strong written and verbal communication skills. * Structured thinker who can bring order and consistency to information * Comfortable coordinating with multiple stakeholders. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office and collaboration tools (e.g., SharePoint, Teams). * Ability to organise work and manage multiple tasks. * Interest in change management, transformation, PMO or business analysis. **Desirable Criteria:** * Experience in the Construction Industry or Trade Background ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer.***

Support + Support the Change Lead in coordinating readiness activities across teams. + Assist in identifying impacts to people, processes and systems. + Help prepare readiness checklists, stakeholder maps, and cutover support materials. + Gather information from functions to track readiness status, risks and dependencies. * Change Framework \& Documentation + Use and maintain Change Framework templates + Support quality checks on documentation before submission to the CCC. + Help update templates and guidance materials based on lessons learned and feedback. + Maintain SharePoint folders, version control and documentation standards. * Communications \& Stakeholder Engagement + Draft communications, updates and briefing materials for impacted teams. + Support workshop preparation and facilitation (slides, notes, logistics). + Help track stakeholder questions, concerns and follow\-up actions. * Change Control \& Governance Support + Assist with data collection for impact scoring, risk profiles, and dependencies. + Track mitigation plans, decision conditions and follow\-up actions. + Maintain the change decision log and ensure it is kept up to date. * Portfolio \& Reporting Support + Assist PMO with data inputs for portfolio dashboards and reporting. + Track small\-scale changes and help identify collisions or overlaps. + Maintain trackers for readiness, risks, actions and adoption progress. * Continuous Improvement + Capture lessons learned and contribute to process improvements. + Suggest enhancements to templates, tools or ways of working. + Take part in developing Mercury’s maturing Change Function. + Conduct trend analysis on change initiatives approved and rejected **Essential Criteria** * Graduate degree (Business, HR, Communications, Engineering, Data, or related field). * Strong written and verbal communication skills. * Structured thinker who can bring order and consistency to information * Comfortable coordinating with multiple stakeholders. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office and collaboration tools (e.g., SharePoint, Teams). * Ability to organise work and manage multiple tasks. * Interest in change management, transformation, PMO or business analysis. **Desirable Criteria** * Experience in the Construction Industry or Trade Background ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer.*** Apply Now

title: Clinical Pharmacist
description: **Pharmacist roles available in TCP Homecare \- Excellent work life balance and full training provided.** **Permanent, full time, desk based, office role, no weekends/late nights** Pharmacist required Monday to Friday in Dublin 12 to join the pharmacist team. No late nights, no on\-call and no rostered weekend work (8 to 5/9 to 6\). Lunch break. Full training is provided. **No experience in homecare necessary \- role may suit new graduate.** The pharmacist position is desk based, with a rotation on checking in the fridge and dispensary in the afternoons. This role will appeal to pharmacists wishing to support patients in the home, learn about rare drugs and bespoke services, and maintain a positive work life balance. The role is based in our licensed pharmacy in Clondalkin, Dublin 12, near the red cow roundabout. * Monday to Friday * Finish by 6pm * Professional fees paid * Protected time for training/learning * Pension/Health insurance contribution after 6 months * Staff discount scheme TCP Homecare are a leading provider of Homecare Services on a national scale specialising in supporting patients in their own home, moving away from hospital based care. Join a company with a positive culture, and contribute to our vision of supporting more and more patients in their home. You will be part of a professional, quality focused team, who continually strive to provide the highest clinical care to patients. There are electronic error management, learning management system and document management systems in place and support departments such as customer service, quality, nursing, drivers and warehouse. Experience gained in the homecare setting provides a good steppingstone to careers in Pharma and hospital. This role provides the opportunity to gain experience in a structured quality environment, and to receive training in Good Distribution Practice (GDP), alongside unique pharmacy experience in orphan drugs, oncology, immunology, haemophilia, clinical trials, TPN and IV antibiotics. **Key Qualifications \& Competencies:** Registered as a pharmacist with the Pharmaceutical Society of Ireland (mandatory) Hard working, positive and enthusiastic Flexible and willing to learn Excellent time management and prioritisation skills, works well to deadlines for patients Attention to detail and a strict quality focus A good team player, with reliability and flexibility key requirements Good administration skills and computer skills, to include excel, word, and outlook Good communication and clinical skills with both patients and professional staff, excellent written and verbal English required **Job Scope:** The Clinical Pharmacist is responsible for ensuring pharmaceutical care for patients, in accordance with the standards of the Pharmaceutical Society of Ireland. **Key Responsibilities:** * Work within the pharmacy team to look after patient medication needs in the community. * Clinically screen all prescriptions for appropriateness of treatment. Liaise with hospital prescribers on prescription queries, request ongoing prescriptions monthly. * Set up new patients, organise drug delivery, fridge, pump and ancillaries as required. * Counsel patients on their medications and support with queries. Liaise with medical information departments as required. Respond to medication related queries from both patients and other healthcare professionals. * Work with the wider TCP team of nurses, quality, logistics and customer service to ensure patient needs are met. * Attend regular meetings to further the quality of the service e.g. monthly pharmacy department meetings, weekly collaborative nursing meetings. * Attend training on orphan drug products, haemophilia, oncology with subject matter experts. Complete training on the Learning Management System. * Maintain the department KPIs and run the monthly hospital and client reports. * Assist with daily checking of patient medication and ancillary orders, which involves working within the dispensary and within a temperature controlled environment (fridge unit). * Contribute to quality by embracing near miss and incident reporting via the electronic MEG system, working on associated corrective and preventative actions. * Partake in audits and regulatory inspections as required. * Be responsible for adherence to GDP standards for wholesale supply of medicines. Support the RP in the maintenance of the Quality Management System. * Participate in clinical trials, in accordance with the standards of Good Clinical Practice. Responsibility for drug accountability and completion of dispensing logs and clinical trial documentation. * Collaborate with the procurement officer to oversee the ordering of rare and orphan drugs. * Maintain professional competency and keep up to date with current developments in current area of practice. Adhere to the guidelines, laws, code of conduct and policies of the Pharmaceutical Society of Ireland. * **What We Offer in Return:** Competitive annual salary 24 days of annual leave for full year, rising with service Pension Scheme Health Insurance Life Assurance A supportive and friendly working environment Regular training courses and opportunities to up skill Promotional opportunities Refer a Friend scheme Social events

title: Clinical Trial Associate

Dublin, D22 K2R3**** Learn more about Grifols

description: Every day, Global Payments makes it possible for millions of people to move money between buyers and sellers using our payments solutions for credit, debit, prepaid and merchant services. Every day, Global Payments makes it possible for millions of people to move money between buyers and sellers using our payments solutions for credit, debit, prepaid and merchant services. Our worldwide team helps over 3 million companies, more than 1,300 financial institutions and over 600 million cardholders grow with confidence and achieve amazing results. We are driven by our passion for success and we are proud to deliver best\-in\-class payment technology and software solutions. Join our dynamic team and make your mark on the payments technology landscape of tomorrow. **Cloud Infrastructure Engineer** **Summary of This Role** We are seeking a highly skilled Infrastructure Engineer to join our Technology Operations group in Warsaw. This is a hands\-on engineering role with strong elements of operational engagement and technical ownership. The successful candidate will provide expertise across Google Cloud Platform (GCP), VM Infrastructure, Terraform, Kubernetes, and configuration management tools (Ansible or equivalent), while helping to define and drive Infrastructure standards, resilience, and reliability. The role spans build, run and operations of our cloud \& vm environment, supporting the full lifecycle from infrastructure\-as\-code through to day\-to\-day operations, on\-call, and continuous improvement initiatives. **What Part Will You Play?** * Implements and operates GCP infrastructure using Terraform (IaC) and Kubernetes (GKE / container orchestration). * Owns infrastructure configuration management (Ansible or equivalent). * Ensures security, scalability, and high availability across infrastructure platforms. * Supports cloud cost optimisation and capacity planning. * Participates in the on\-call rotation, providing tier\-3/4 support for critical infrastructure services. * Participates in incident response and root\-cause analysis for infrastructure and cloud issues. * Drives automation of monitoring, alerting, and remediation workflows. * Partners with cross functional teams to align delivery models (Kanban for BAU, Agile for projects). * Jira governance for Infrastructure projects — workflow / agile methodology * Produces technical assessments / documentation upholding standards and providing recommendations for management. * Deploys and maintains Kubernetes clusters (GKE). * Configures namespaces, RBAC, ingresses controllers, and services mesh as required. * Builds and deploys containerised workloads; manages scaling, upgrades, and patching. * Troubleshoots pod, node, and networking issues in production clusters. * Writes and maintains Ansible playbooks and roles for VM and cluster configuration. * Automates patching, security hardening, and system updates. * Standardises environment builds and enforce consistency across dev/test/prod. **What Are We Looking For in This Role?** ----------------------------------------- **Minimum Qualifications** * Bachelor's Degree * 3\+ years’ experience as an Infrastructure Engineer, SRE, or Cloud Engineer in enterprise environments. * Proven expertise in Google Cloud Platform (GCP). * Strong skills in VM workloads, Terraform (IaC) and Kubernetes (GKE or equivalent). * Hands\-on with Ansible (or equivalent configuration management). * Broad knowledge of networking, databases, Linux systems, and hybrid infrastructure. * Experience with monitoring, observability, and incident response tooling. * Polish (at least B1\+ \- both spoken and written) and English (at least C1\) **Preferred Qualifications** * GCP Professional Cloud Architect or equivalent certification or Hands on Experience. * Experience in regulated environments (PCI\-DSS, ISO, SOC2, etc.) an advantage * DevOps / SRE hands on experience **What Are Our Desired Skills and Capabilities?** ------------------------------------------------- * Strong communicator — able to influence both technical engineers and senior stakeholders. * Organised and pragmatic, with strong ownership and accountability. * Collaborative style — balances governance with delivery speed. * Demonstrated success implementing Kanban and Agile delivery within Infra/Cloud Ops. * Ability to document and enforce technical standards. * Proven track record of improving operational performance and cross\-team delivery. This exciting **12\-month program** is designed to give graduates exposure to both sales and solution engineering. The promotion window opens from **month 7 onwards.** Our graduate program is a fully immersive experience, combining comprehensive learning paths with in\-role experience. You will be assigned experienced **Sales Development Buddys** who will provide hands\-on coaching, invite you to join their discovery and qualification calls, giving you the confidence to pick up the phone to reach out to your first salesforce customers. You will also be paired with experienced **Solution Engineer Buddies** during the SE Rotation who will involve you in their live deals. Throughout the programme, you will gain exposure to senior sales and solution engineering professionals during our inspiration sessions. By joining Salesforce, you will have the opportunity to lay the foundation for a **fast\-paced, successful career** . We are a proud member of the **Fortune 100 Best Places to Work** list for the past 14 years and a consistent winner of **Great Place to Work** awards. Who are we looking for? * You are a 2025 or 2026 graduate (minimum bachelor’s degree) from all academic backgrounds * You are interested in the impact technology can have on the world and business * You are a creative problem\-solver * You are a relationship builder and possess a growth mindset * You are curious, self\-motivated, and focused on continuous learning * You thrive in a team environment * You have excellent interpersonal skills * You are goal\-oriented and comfortable in a fast\-paced environment * You are fluent in English \& Dutch What does our EMEA Graduate Program look like? **The Program Is Structured As Follows** Introduction Month During your first month, you will receive extensive onboarding enablement through a Salesforce\-style academy, where you will: * Become immersed in the culture of Salesforce and gain a deep understanding of our values * Develop an understanding of our solutions, technologies, and tools * Learn critical professional skills for your career at Salesforce * Follow a structured learning calendar with sessions and hands\-on workshops led by experts and program alumni Rotations During the Graduate Program, graduates on both the Sales and Solution Engineering Paths will follow the same curriculum and complete the same rotations. Essentially you will gain exposure to both the **Sales and Solution Engineering Roles** . The structure of each rotation is similar: you will spend **60% of your time** immersed in the role with clear tasks, targets, and stakeholder alignment, while the remaining **40%** will focus on role\-specific learning. You will be paired with experienced buddies in each path. If your first rotation (months 2 through 4\) is in **Sales** , you will then join the **Solution Engineering Rotation** from months 5 through 7, and vice versa. Over these six months, you will have built the fundamental skills for these complementary disciplines. Having gone deeper on your solution knowledge, learning how to demo and having exposure to later stages of the sales cycle during the solution engineering rotation will set you up for sucess as you promote throught the various sales roles \- SDR, BDR, AE and beyond. Throughout the rotations, there will be **self\-paced certifications and skills assessments** . You must meet all eligibility criteria before you can **panel for promotion** . Examples of what you can expect during the rotations **Sales Graduate Rotation** * Learn Salesforce’s best\-in\-class prospecting and qualification methodologies * Learn how to use social, digital, video, chat, and phone platforms to effectively engage Salesforce prospects * Collaborate with Sales Development Specialists to run campaigns and help build sales pipeline for the EMEA Sales organisation Solution Engineering Graduate Rotation * Learn our solutions at a deeper, more technical level * Work closely with the Solution Engineering organisation, supporting the creation of cutting\-edge customer demonstrations * Learn the art of customer discovery and how to create a compelling customer point of view What’s in it for you? * A clear and structured career path within our Sales organisation * The opportunity to work in an international team within a nurturing environment * Access to our diverse internal employee groups * A comprehensive benefits package * Relocation budget and guidance throughout your relocation process * An additional seven days of paid time off for charitable causes – learn more about our 1\-1\-1 model Map your future for the coming years With the **EMEA Graduate Program – Sales Path** , you can continue to progress: * After your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Sales Development Representative, Business Development Representative and then Account Executive, where you will position our solutions to potential customers. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * With our strong mentoring culture, we strive to develop industry\-leading Solution Engineering leaders. This is a phenomenal opportunity to build international experience within an organisation that has transformed the enterprise software market through cloud computing. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. title: Data Reliability Engineer
description: **Overview** Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation. Our culture is intellectually driven and highly collaborative, bringing together researchers, engineers, and traders to design and deploy impactful strategies in our systematic trading environment. To meet the unique challenges of global markets, Susquehanna applies machine learning and advanced quantitative research to vast datasets in order to uncover actionable insights and build effective strategies. By uniting deep market expertise with cutting\-edge technology, we excel in solving complex problems and pushing boundaries together. We’re looking for a Data Reliability Engineer to help keep our trading and data platforms running seamlessly. In this role, you’ll be the guardian of our data pipelines — ensuring that trading critical Airflow workflows and Python\-based jobs run smoothly, on time, and with precision. You’ll dive deep into incidents when they happen, diagnose issues quickly, and make the fixes that keep downstream systems healthy and reliable. Your work will directly support the speed and stability our trading teams depend on every day. You’ll collaborate closely with quantitative researchers, data and reliability engineers, and developers to review changes, manage releases, and strengthen our overall deployment practices. Beyond keeping things running, you’ll help make them better — improving monitoring, alerts, and recovery processes while automating wherever possible. This role is perfect for someone who thrives on ownership, loves solving complex operational puzzles, and is passionate about building robust, high\-performance data systems that never miss a beat. **In This Role You Will** * Ensure Platform Reliability \- Monitor and maintain trading\-critical Airflow DAGs and Python\-based pipelines, ensuring jobs run on time and within SLAs * Incident Response \& Recovery \- Triage, troubleshoot, and resolve failures quickly; validate downstream impacts and maintain tested rollback/recovery procedures * Change \& Release Management \- Act as a release gatekeeper—review code/config changes, enforce safe deployment standards, and coordinate risk\-aware releases via Git(lab) and Octopus Deploy * Collaboration \& Communication \- Partner with quants and engineers to assess change impacts, document runbooks, and communicate operational updates and risks * Continuous Improvement \- Enhance monitoring, alerting, and automation; track KPIs and drive initiatives that strengthen platform resilience and reduce incident recurrence **What We're Looking For** * Degree in a technical or business discipline or equivalent industry experience of 1\+ years * Dem o nstrated experience with Python or equivalent language * Excellent analytical \& troubleshooting skills, self\-motivated and curious * Willing to work shift hours, to cover early and late responsibilities (alternating) * Experience with Change Management, Incident Management Procedures * Experience of technical documentation \& support cases **Desirable** * Financial Trade Floor experience is a plus but not essential, training will be provided. * Knowledge of system monitoring tools such as Check\_MK, Splunk , ELK * Awareness of modern distributed application systems and the glue that binds them \- messaging and database systems If you're a recruiting agency and want to partner with us, please reach out to recruiting@sig.com . Any resume or referral submitted in the absence of a signed agreement will not be eligible for an agency fee.

title: DevOps Engineer - IAM Cloud
description: Optum is a global organization that delivers care, aided by technology to help millions of people live healthier lives. The work you do with our team will directly improve health outcomes by connecting people with the care, pharmacy benefits, data and resources they need to feel their best. Here, you will find a culture guided by diversity and inclusion, talented peers, comprehensive benefits and career development opportunities. Come make an impact on the communities we serve as you help us advance health equity on a global scale. Join us to start **Caring. Connecting. Growing together.** IAM Cloud Engineering team at UHG specializes in developing capabilities that enable our cloud enterprise applications and protects access to our cloud assets, data and services. As a member of this innovative agile team, you will collaborate with others to maintain and optimize these capabilities to secure our growing dynamic cloud computing environments. Engineers on this team will work with many popular vendor and open\-source technologies, internally developed applications and cloud native services. You will be challenged to provide technical and thought leadership and to succeed in this role, you need to be eager to learn, able to work through ambiguity, problem solve and communicate effectively. We are looking for a security\-focused DevOps engineer that is a self\-starter and demonstrates a solid understanding of the impact of their work. One who is continuously looking for opportunities to simplify and optimize cloud IAM technologies and processes. Careers with Optum offer flexible work arrangements and individuals who live and work in the Republic of Ireland will have the opportunity to split their monthly work hours between our Dublin or Letterkenny office and telecommuting from a home\-based office in a hybrid work model. **What You Will Do** * Define and develop solutions to enforce least privilege access policies * Implement and manage access controls, including role\-based access control (RBAC) * Monitoring, logging, and analyzing system performance using Azure operations suite to maintain system health and optimize costs * Develop and implement disaster recovery and data backup strategies and solutions to maintain business continuity * Collaborate with development teams to enable CI/CD pipelines for efficient software deployment using Azure services * Establish and maintain cloud identity governance frameworks * Develop and maintain guardrails to enforce/measure IAM cloud compliance with regulatory requirements * Conduct periodic access reviews and monitor user activity * Lead cloud migration and modernization initiatives * Advise on best practices for cloud security, reliability, and scalability * Drive innovation and discovery efforts around new Azure features and services * Participates in the development of prototypes and large\-scale proof of concepts * Maintain knowledgeable around current application security controls You will be rewarded and recognized for your performance in an environment that will challenge you and give you clear direction on what it takes to succeed in your role, as well as providing development for other roles you may be interested in. **What You Will Bring** If you don't meet every single requirement, don't let that hold you back \- we're just as excited about potential as we are about qualifications and experience, and you could be exactly who we're looking for. * Proven solid hands\-on experience in deploying and operating resources in Azure and AWS * Demonstrated solid understanding of Infrastructure as Code (IAC) and experience working with CI/CD technologies such as git and Terraform * Proven solid hands\-on experience with Azure Identity and Access Management concepts and security frameworks * Proven solid hands\-on experience with Entra ID and Entra Connect Sync * Demonstrated solid hands\-on experience in developing and managing Organization policies * Proven experience with federation technologies and protocols such as SAML, OAuth, OIDC, etc. * Proven ample experience operating with cloud platforms, APIs and event\-driven automation * Demonstrated proficiency with popular scripting and programming language frameworks such as Python, Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK, Microsoft Graph API, Shell Scripting languages * Demonstrated passion for innovation and enjoyment of the challenges of creating something new * Proven technical and thought leadership skills, with the ability to collaborate with others to successfully deliver large, complex initiatives * Demonstrated solid written and verbal communication skills **Other Useful Skills And Experience Include** * Certifications in CompTIA Security\+, Azure Administrator Associate, Azure Security Engineer Associate, Microsoft Security, Compliance, and Identity Fundamentals, Azure Fundamentals, AWS Cloud Practitioner, AWS Security (Specialty), CCSP or similar * Proven previous experience working in/with AWS Identity and Access Management, multi\-factor authentication, user provisioning, service principal governance/management, privileged Identity management (PIM), single sign\-on (SSO), domain management * Proven experience in Cloud IAM functionality across multiple public cloud services (IAM Roles, Permissions and Policies) * Demonstrated ability to implement and manage Cloud Infrastructure Entitlement Management (CIEM) to meet organizational access and security objectives * Demonstrated advocacy for CI/CD and DevOps best practices * Demonstrated fundamental understanding of PHI/PII data and the restrictions around it using limited data sets/de\-identified data for analysis/development and testing where applicable **What We Offer** * Opportunities for professional development * Inclusive and supportive team culture * Key benefits: Private health insurance, wellness programs, matching pension contribution, lunch provided by the company, training opportunities, employee donations matching and others **Please note you must currently be eligible to work and remain indefinitely without any restrictions in the country to which you are making an application. Proof will be required to support your application.** *At UnitedHealth Group, our mission is to help people live healthier lives and make the health system work better for everyone. We believe everyone\-of every race, gender, sexuality, age, location and income\-deserves the opportunity to live their healthiest life. Today, however, there are still far too many barriers to good health which are disproportionately experienced by people of color, historically marginalized groups and those with lower incomes. We are committed to mitigating our impact on the environment and enabling and delivering equitable care that addresses health disparities and improves health outcomes \- an enterprise priority reflected in our mission.* *Diversity creates a healthier atmosphere: Optum is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, disability, religion, age, race, and membership of the Traveller community, or any other characteristic protected by law. Optum is a drug\-free workplace. © 2026 Optum Services (Ireland) Limited. All rights reserved.* \#BBMEMEA
