title: Junior Health and Safety Advisor
description: **Are you looking to become part of a collaborative team whilst maximising your potential to grow and develop within your career? Apply today.** **The Role.** GMC are currently looking for a **Junior Health and Safety Advisor** to join our team.The successful Health and Safety Advisor will be supporting the delivery of projects across all aspects of the business from a Health and Safety point of view. A strong ethos on health and safety, compliance, reporting, and documentation are essential qualities. **Key Duties and Responsibilities will include but are not limited to:** * Provide health and safety training to employees including but not limited to manual handling, toolbox talks and safety induction. * Previous experience conducting safety inspections and audits. * Approaches accident prevention and protection of the environment in a positive manner with the ability to initiate and maintain this positive manner with effective and efficient measures to raise safety, health and environmental standards and awareness. * Assist in the preparation of reports on occurrences and provide statistical information to management. * Implement the company's health, safety and environmental policy, procedures, and management systems. * Assist in the maintenance of Health \& Safety KPIs to help drive improvement in all related matters. * Support management in the development of H\&S procedures, policies, and programs. * Work as part of the HSQE team to ensure the provision of a safe and productive work environment. * Always seek to improve the standard of Health \& Safety in a practical manner within the company management system and on specific sites. * Monitor and enforce the implementation of Health \& Safety Management System. * Identify site specific hazards and ensure they are addressed in the site documentation and in a practical manner on the site. * Ensure clear lines of communication are maintained to the H\&S Manager. * Ensure the site folders and statutory records are being maintained. * Ensure strict adherence to site rules by all. * Assist site management to comply with statutory H\&S requirements. * Assist Health \& Safety Manager to develop and implement Risk assessments and improvements to the Safe Systems of Work. * Assist H\&S Manager and Site management to coordinate the safe systems of work for all. * Carry out H\&S auditing on site and address non\-conformances as they are identified with site management and contractors. * Contribute to accident and incident investigation. * Identify training needs on site for GMC personnel and contractors. * Ad hoc tasks as needed by the wider business. **The Candidate: Required Experience and Competencies:** * Full clean driver's license essential. * A 3rd level qualification in Health and Safety is desirable. * 1\+ years experience in a H\&S role. * Civils, drainage or utilities (water \& electricity) experience. * Confident, motivated and can work independently as well as part of a team. * Ability to work under pressure with strict deadlines. * Time management abilities to meet targets. * Good communication, interpersonal, and customer service skills. * Integrity and respect for confidentiality and privacy. * Strong relationship builder and ability to leverage key relationships across the full spectrum of project stakeholders and statutory bodies. * Ability to manage risk and implement control measures. * Able to work and build strong relationships with contractors from various backgrounds and trades. **Become a part of something bigger.** At the heart of GMC is our people, from engineers through to customer service and we aim to work collaboratively towards our common goals. GMC is an equal opportunity employer, and we aim to recruit and retain like\-minded people from diverse backgrounds with a wide range of expertise. If you require any specific accommodations, we will make every reasonable effort to support you throughout the recruitment and selection process. Please contact us at careers@gmcirl.com. In GMC we foster a work culture that promotes collaboration and learning, building an environment where our people have the capacity to maximise their potential whilst gaining the opportunity to continuously develop. **Our Future.** Since the founding of GMC in 1981 by Gerry McCloskey, GMC has grown to be Ireland's leading Civils and Utilities company and the future for GMC is bright. We have engaged with our staff and other stakeholders to agree and publish our core values. One of our core values is to *"Focus on the Future"* . Our growth to date reflects the confidence that our customers have in our service delivery, in the innovative approach that we bring to how we do our work, and to our culture of safety and quality. We have ambitious plans to develop our footprint in Ireland and overseas but understand that every decision we make contributes to people's quality of life throughout generations, and we don't take that responsibility lightly as we build Ireland's infrastructure of tomorrow, today.

title: Junior IT Support Technician
description: **The Role.** We are looking for an IT Support Technician to join our internal IT team and provide first\-line support to staff across multiple offices throughout Ireland. This is a great opportunity for someone with around 1\-2 years' IT support experience who is looking to build their career in a busy, hands\-on environment. The role is focused on day\-to\-day user support, responding to tickets, troubleshooting common IT issues, setting up devices, and helping staff both remotely and on site. You will work closely with the wider IT team and play an important role in keeping users productive and IT services running smoothly. **Key Duties and Responsibilities will include but are not limited to:** **Key Responsibilities:** * Provide first\-line IT support to staff across multiple office locations * Log, triage, and manage incidents and service requests through the IT ticketing system * Support users by email, phone, remote support tools, and in person * Troubleshoot day\-to\-day issues relating to laptops, desktops, printers, mobile phones, tablets, and other end\-user devices * Set up, deploy, and maintain user devices and equipment * Assist with user account administration and general Microsoft 365 / Windows support tasks * Maintain accurate device and asset records * Escalate more complex issues to senior members of the IT team where required * Assist with routine IT maintenance tasks, software updates, and general housekeeping * Support small IT projects and office visits as needed **The Candidate: Required Experience and Competencies:** * Approximately 1\-2 years' experience in an IT support or service desk role * Good working knowledge of Windows and Microsoft Office / Microsoft 365 * A solid understanding of core IT fundamentals is essential, including IP addressing, DNS and DHCP. Candidates should understand how these technologies enable devices and systems to communicate across a network. * Experience supporting end users with common hardware and software issues * Strong communication skills and a helpful, professional approach * Ability to manage and prioritise workload through a ticketing system * A genuine interest in IT and a willingness to learn and develop * Ability to work well as part of a team and use initiative when required * Full driving licence and own transport, as occasional travel to other office locations may be required. (Travel expenses will be paid per a kilometre travelled) **Desirable** * Exposure to Active Directory, Office 365 administration, basic networking, and remote support tools * Experience with device builds, patching, or asset management * IT certifications such as CompTIA A\+, Network\+, CCNA or Microsoft certifications **What The Role Offers** * A hands\-on opportunity to build broad IT support experience * Exposure to a wide range of technologies and day\-to\-day operational support * The chance to develop your skills within a busy internal IT team * Ongoing learning and practical experience across multiple office environments * A strong culture of continuous learning, with encouragement for team members to pursue relevant certifications and further develop their technical skills **As occasional travel to company locations is required, candidates must hold a full clean driving licence valid for use in Ireland, or be willing to obtain one within the first 6\-9 months of employment.** **Become a part of something bigger.** At the heart of GMC is our people, from engineers through to customer service and we aim to work collaboratively towards our common goals. GMC is an equal opportunity employer, and we aim to recruit and retain like\-minded people from diverse backgrounds with a wide range of expertise. title: Junior QS
description: *Please note: This job requires you to have Irish/UK experience and can not offer any sponsorship* **Job Summary:** Our client is offering an excellent opportunity for a Quantity Surveyor to join a growing business with a clear strategic plan for expansion. Reporting to the Commercial Director and based in East Meath on a hybrid basis, this role will suit someone looking to progress their career in a company with strong long\-term ambition. **Role Description:** * Manage all aspects of quantity surveying for construction projects. * Develop the supply chain. * Oversee project procurement. * Prepare monthly project payment applications and cost reports. * Monitor project costs and agree variations. * Remeasure site works. * Report monthly to senior management on financial performance. * Prepare and present cost and risk analysis. * Manage all aspects of tendering, including financial assessments. * Ensure best value is achieved across all elements. * Audit and report on subcontractor and supplier returns. **Suitable Candidates Should Have:** * Residential experience, with modular or timber frame experience preferred. * A minimum of 2 years’ experience in a similar role. * A third\-level qualification in Construction, Building, or a related field. * Strong communication skills and in\-depth knowledge of construction practices, trades, and techniques. * A proven track record in securing profitable projects. * Excellent IT skills. * A full clean European driving licence and access to transport. * Strong Microsoft Office skills. * The ability to work well as part of a team. * The ability to meet deadlines. * Excellent numeracy and computer skills. * A professional presence, dress, and demeanour. **Salary:** Negotiable, and will include a suitable benefits package.

title: Lecturer/AP in Computer Science, UCD Computer Sci, Perm or Temp
description: **Applications are invited for a permanent or temporary post of a Lecturer/Assistant Professor in Computer Science within UCD School of Computer Science.** The UCD School of Computer Science (ucd.ie/cs) is Ireland's leading computer science school in terms of teaching and research. We offer taught programmes at undergraduate and postgraduate levels, which attract the top students from Ireland and abroad. Our staff are research active and hold key leadership positions in internationally significant research centres such as the Insight Centre for Data Analytics (insight\-centre.org), CeADAR \- the Centre for Applied AI (ceadar.ie), ML Labs Centre for Research Training (ml\-labs.ie), and the UCD Centre for Cybersecurity and Cybercrime Investigation (www.ucd.ie/cci). School staff are actively involved in a number of EU projects in areas such as Digital Health, Cybersecurity, ML \& AI and HCI. The School is committed to excellence in teaching and research and to supporting the Information \& Communications Technology sector in Ireland. The new Assistant Professor/s will be expected to teach on the BSc and MSc programmes provided by the School, in particular to contribute to undergraduate and graduate teaching in cybersecurity and/or software engineering, and to contribute to the research activity of the School. Candidates with expertise in one or more of the following subject areas are particularly encouraged to apply: Cybersecurity, Software Engineering and Distributed Systems. **Important Note for applicants:** Please state clearly in the cover letter of your application any preferences you may have with regard to the available levels of appointment. If not otherwise specified, it will be assumed you wish your application to be considered for either level. **95 Lecturer/Asst Professor Above the Bar (7943\) Salary Scale: €65,401 \- €103,564 Per Annum** Appointment will be made on scale and in accordance with the Department of Finance guidelines **Closing date: 12:00 noon (local Irish time) on 01 May 2026\.** Applications must be submitted by the closing date and time specified. Any applications which are still in progress at the closing time of 12:00 noon (Local Irish Time) on the specified closing date will be cancelled automatically by the system. UCD are unable to accept late applications. UCD do not require assistance from Recruitment Agencies. Any CVs submitted by Recruitment Agencies will be returned. **Note:** Hours of work for academic staff are those as prescribed under Public Service Agreements. For further information, please follow the link: https://www.ucd.ie/hr/t4media/Academic Contract.pdf Prior to application, further information (including application procedure) should be obtained from the Work at UCD website: https://www.ucd.ie/workatucd/jobs/ **Desired Skills and Experience** Communication and interpersonal skills, Ability to deliver high quality teaching, A track record of research achievement, Ability to work as part of a team, Fluency in English, Experience of mentoring or supervision

description: **Maintenance Supervisor** Carlton Hotel Blanchardstown \& Carlton Hotel Dublin Airport – Dublin **Why Join Us** * Competitive salary (DOE) * Performance Bonus Scheme (linked to individual and team performance) * Free on\-site parking * Structured, supportive, and performance\-driven environment * Staff meals on duty \& uniform provided * Employee engagement initiatives and social events **About the Role** Carlton Hotels are seeking a Maintenance Supervisor to support the Facilities Manager in overseeing maintenance operations across our hotel group. performance and hotel results * Clear, measurable KPIs — you know exactly how to earn it * Key focus areas: * Response times \& job completion efficiency * Preventative maintenance delivery * Guest satisfaction (maintenance\-related feedback) * Health \& safety compliance * Cost control and resource management * Direct impact on earnings based on performance and results **Key Responsibilities** * Carry out and supervise day\-to\-day maintenance across hotel facilities (electrical, plumbing, HVAC, general repairs) * Conduct regular inspections to identify maintenance needs and potential risks * Respond promptly to maintenance requests from guests and departments * Support and implement planned preventative maintenance programmes * Maintain accurate records of work orders, inspections, and repairs * Liaise with external contractors and oversee works on\-site * Ensure compliance with all health, safety, fire, and statutory regulations * Monitor stock levels of maintenance supplies and equipment * Assist with event setup and breakdown as required * Support emergency procedures and respond effectively to incidents * Work collaboratively with Front Office, Accommodation, and other departments **The Ideal Candidate** Essential * Minimum 2 years’ experience in maintenance, ideally within a hotel or hospitality environment * Strong technical knowledge across general maintenance (electrical, plumbing, HVAC, etc.) * Proven ability to troubleshoot and resolve maintenance issues efficiently * Good understanding of health \& safety and compliance requirements * Strong organisational and problem\-solving skills * Ability to work independently and as part of a team * Good communication and interpersonal skills * Basic computer skills for record\-keeping Desirable * Previous supervisory or team\-leading experience * Experience in a multi\-site or hotel group environment * Knowledge of maintenance management systems **Personal Attributes** * Hands\-on, proactive, and solution\-focused * High attention to detail * Calm and efficient under pressure * Strong work ethic and reliability * Flexible approach to working hours (including weekends if required) * title: Marketing Intern
description: **Summer Marketing Internship** – In\-Office (Naas, 3 Days/Week) Location: Naas, Co. Kildare Duration: 3 Months (Summer 2026\) Schedule: 3 Days per Week (Tuesday–Thursday preferred) Are you a student or recent graduate looking to gain hands\-on marketing experience this summer? We're looking for a marketing intern with real design skills and a genuine hunger to learn. This is a must for the role. * A genuine eye for design and visual communication * Confident using social media platforms (especially Instagram, TikTok, and LinkedIn) * A can\-do attitude, willingness to learn and someone who wants to be part of the team * Ability to take feedback, work independently and ask questions when you don't know something * Available for 3 days per week in our Naas office over the summer period * Organised and reliable with deadlines * CapCut experience is a bonus but not essential, we'll teach you if you're keen **What You’ll Gain** * Mentorship and support from an experienced team * Agency insight and client exposure * A reference or completion letter for your CV * What a marketing agency does day to day. **To Apply** Send us your CV to hello@elevatemarketing.ie and a short note about why you're interested, plus any links to past content, design work, or social media examples (optional but encouraged). title: Marketing Technology Analyst
description: No Relocation Assistance Offered Job Number \#172769 \- Dublin, Dublin, Ireland **Who We Are** Colgate\-Palmolive Company is a global consumer products company operating in over 200 countries specializing in Oral Care, Personal Care, Home Care, Skin Care, and Pet Nutrition. Our products are trusted in more households than any other brand in the world, making us a household name! Join Colgate\-Palmolive, a caring, innovative growth company reimagining a healthier future for people, their pets, and our planet. Guided by our core values—Caring, Inclusive, and Courageous—we foster a culture that inspires our people to achieve common goals. Together, let's build a brighter, healthier future for all. **Marketing Technology Analyst, Digital Marketing GIT EMEA** **Help us transform the digital experience for pets and the people who care for them.** We are looking for a high\-energy **Marketing Technology** **Analyst** to join our GIT EMEA team. You will **lead** technology enablement for Data Acquisition, Marketing Automation and Omni\-Channel Personalisation for Hill’s EMEA across the pet and vet ecosystem. We aim to create a **seamless, personalized digital experience** that supports veterinarians in their practice and empowers pet parents in their journey. By harmonizing data across every touchpoint, we ensure that every interaction—whether in a clinic or on a smartphone—is relevant, timely, and impactful. **The Mission** 1P CRM data acquisition and activation is a key strategic driver for Hill’s EMEA. You will be the bridge between cutting\-edge technology and real\-world business value, working at the center of the Hill’s EMEA team, as part of the Global IT team, to craft a world\-class digital journey for vets and pet parents. **What You’ll Do** * Master the Data Lifecycle: Maintain a deep, end\-to\-end understanding of data flows across the entire ecosystem. You will ensure that data moves seamlessly between platforms with zero loss, maintaining high integrity from ingestion to activation. * Partner with Global Digital Applications teams to implement MarTech solutions across the entire professional and consumer journey. * Partner with the Hill’s EMEA team to drive acquisition, activation, and harmonization of first\-party data to fuel hyper\-personalized engagement. * Empower Local Markets: You’ll guide local markets on leveraging global platforms while ensuring their specific requirements are translated into technical reality. * Partner with CRM Managers: Work hand\-in\-hand with CRM leads to translate strategic objectives into technical requirements, ensuring data, journeys, and campaigns are built for success. * Be the Tech Ambassador: Translate "tech\-speak" into business value, driving tool adoption and ensuring stakeholders are aligned and inspired. * Safeguard the Experience: Lead regression testing across the ecosystem to ensure technical updates never compromise the user experience. * Document \& Train: Create the "source of truth" documentation and training materials to clarify roles and keep technical knowledge flowing across teams. * Manage the Flow: Navigate agile projects with ease, managing multiple assignments without losing sight of the details. **Who You Are** * Marketing\-Centric: You have a proven track record in Marketing, CRM and Media projects. You understand the "why" behind a campaign and how to speak the language of brand managers. * Technical Specialist: You are hands\-on with data modeling, complex analysis, and activation workflows. You don't just see data; you see the architecture required to move it. * A Solutions Hunter: You don’t just find bugs; you find better ways to work. You are self\-motivated, comfortable with ambiguity, and love to troubleshoot and experiment. * A Master Communicator: You can explain a roadmap to a developer and a business case to a Director with equal clarity. * Technically Sharp: You understand the "how" behind the "what," especially regarding data ingestion and harmonized ecosystems. **Your Toolkit** * Education: Degree in Business, IT, Computer Science, or Engineering. * Tech Literacy: Solid grasp of APIs, data modeling, data analysis, workflow and the digital marketing ecosystem. * Agile Mindset: Proven track record of managing digital initiatives and technology enablement in an agile environment. **Bonus Points For:** * A background in Email Marketing and activation strategy. * Hands\-on experience with Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Salesforce Data360, Gigya (Customer Data Cloud), and Snowflake. * Experience with CDP, Adobe Target, AEM, GA4 * Experience with Clean rooms; Infosum, Liveramp, Amazon **Knowledge of SQL, JavaScript, HTML, or CSS.** **Our Commitment to Inclusion** Our journey begins with our people—developing strong talent with diverse backgrounds and perspectives to best serve our consumers around the world and fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels a true sense of belonging. We are dedicated to ensuring that each individual can be their authentic self, is treated with respect, and is empowered by leadership to contribute meaningfully to our business. **Equal Opportunity Employer** Colgate is an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, ethnicity, age, disability, marital status, veteran status (United States positions), or any other characteristic protected by law. Reasonable accommodation during the application process is available for persons with disabilities. Please complete this request form should you require accommodation.

title: Model Risk Management Governance, Associate
description: **About This Role** The mission of the **Risk and Quantitative Analysis (RQA)** function is to help BlackRock manage its fiduciary and enterprise risks and provide independent oversight. While executing this mission, RQA provides quantitative analysis to many of BlackRock’s businesses and helps promote the spread of best practice across the firm. The **Enterprise Risk** team within RQA oversees those risks which directly impact the corporate entity (i.e. BlackRock, Inc., or one of its subsidiaries), rather than the fiduciary risks assumed by funds/clients, such as investment risks and counterparty risks. The team’s focus includes but is not limited to operational risk, technology, vendor, model and market risk, stress testing for market risk and the quantitative estimation of capital adequacy requirements. The enterprise risk function remains closely engaged with the evolving regulatory environment and supervisory expectations, to ensure that BlackRock’s internal risk and control frameworks remain consistent with these, and to co\-ordinate regulatory initiatives from an RQA perspective (for example, capital and liquidity assessments). The **Global Model Risk Management** function within Enterprise Risk team is responsible for developing the firm\-wide Model Risk Management framework and the implementation of the framework across the firm. Key responsibilities of the team include performing independent review of model risk related activities across the firm, working with the model owners and proving oversight and assessment of their model risk procedures to ensure that they are comprehensive, rigorous and effective and follow the firm\-wide and regulatory requirements. The role will be based onsite in our Dublin office. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist with the implementation of the firm\-wide Model Risk Management Policy. * Support the execution of governance activities including activities covered by relevant sub\-committees of the Enterprise Risk Committee (ERC). * Drive operational efficiency through process improvements or automation (i.e. tracking risk metrics, issues, performance issues, errors, internal audit findings and any issues raised by regulators, managing incidents and risk \& control issues. * Promote the development and implementation of the model risk management activities in the model risk management system in compliance with the Policies and regulatory guidelines. * Prepare report on the status of Model risk to the appropriate risk management oversight committees on a periodic basis and as required. * Responding to Model related inquiries from regulators or auditors. **Knowledge/Experience/ Competencies** * Minimum of 3 years’ works experience in the financial industry. * Undergraduate/ Master’s in Computer Science, Mathematics, Finance, Financial or Engineering. * Excellent communication, negotiation and problem\-solving skills * Ability to draft governance documents and committee materials with clarity and precision * Experience in quantitative Model reviews or process improvements, change management or project lifecycle management * Strong interpersonal and organizational skills and attention to details. * Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities simultaneously and can prioritize and escalate as appropriate * Programming experience in one or more environments such as R, Python and SQL * Proven experience working on M365 tools (specifically Excel \& PowerPoint) and platform such as Power Automate, Power BI and Copilot Studio **Our Benefits** To help you stay energized, engaged and inspired, we offer a wide range of employee benefits including: retirement investment and tools designed to help you in building a sound financial future; access to education reimbursement; comprehensive resources to support your physical health and emotional well\-being; family support programs; and Flexible Time Off (FTO) so you can relax, recharge and be there for the people you care about. **Our hybrid work model** BlackRock’s hybrid work model is designed to enable a culture of collaboration and apprenticeship that enriches the experience of our employees, while supporting flexibility for all. Employees are currently required to work at least 4 days in the office per week, with the flexibility to work from home 1 day a week. Some business groups may require more time in the office due to their roles and responsibilities. We remain focused on increasing the impactful moments that arise when we work together in person – aligned with our commitment to performance and innovation. As a new joiner, you can count on this hybrid model to accelerate your learning and onboarding experience here at BlackRock. **About BlackRock** At BlackRock, we are all connected by one mission: to help more and more people experience financial well\-being. Our clients, and the people they serve, are saving for retirement, paying for their children’s educations, buying homes and starting businesses. Their investments also help to strengthen the global economy: support businesses small and large; finance infrastructure projects that connect and power cities; and facilitate innovations that drive progress. This mission would not be possible without our smartest investment – the one we make in our employees. It’s why we’re dedicated to creating an environment where our colleagues feel welcomed, valued and supported with networks, benefits and development opportunities to help them thrive. For additional information on BlackRock, please visit @blackrock \| Twitter: @blackrock \| LinkedIn: www.linkedin.com/company/blackrock BlackRock is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to age, disability, race, religion, sex, sexual orientation and other protected characteristics at law.

title: Network Engineer
description: **Location: Dublin, Ireland** **Duration: 12 months** **Engagement Type: Contract\- with opportunities for employment after** **Role Overview** Junior Network Engineer will provide technical support to wider Technology teams, ensuring smooth day\-to\-day network operations. The role focuses on supporting enterprise Aruba\-based network environments, including both wired and wireless infrastructure, with a strong emphasis on ClearPass\-driven access control and segmentation. **Required Technical Skills** **Aruba CX Networking (L2 \& L3\)** Implementation and troubleshooting Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) OSPF routing **Switching \& Stacking Technologies** VSF (Virtual Switching Framework) VSX (Virtual Switching Extension) **Core Networking Concepts** Strong understanding of IPv4 subnetting VLAN segmentation and design principles **Aruba ClearPass** 802\.1X service profiles Integration with Aruba CX 6300 edge switches Dynamic segmentation Deployment of colourless ports **Wireless Networking** Aruba WLAN (AOS8\) deployments Troubleshooting wireless environments **Key Responsibilities** * Network Onboarding \& Configuration * Assist with onboarding of new clients via ClearPass (wired and wireless) * Domain PCs, IoT devices, and medical devices * Support expansion and configuration of Aruba access points * Configure VLANs and IP subnets as directed by Network Design Authority **Troubleshooting \& Support** * Diagnose and resolve wired and wireless connectivity issues * Support IT teams with onboarding of complex devices (e.g. medical/IoT) * Troubleshoot general network issues across LAN and WLAN environments **Operations \& Maintenance** * Perform day\-to\-day network operational tasks, including: * Device backups * Monitoring via IMC (Intelligent Management Center) * Network health checks * Replace faulty hardware (e.g. Aruba CX 6300 VSF switch stacks) **Upgrades \& Improvements** * Plan and implement software upgrades for: * Aruba AOS8 WLAN infrastructure * Wired network environment

title: Network Planning Analyst
description: **Description** Ryanair is currently recruiting for a Network Planning Analyst to join Europe’s biggest airline group. This is an excellent time to join Ryanair as we look to grow to over 800 aircraft and 300m guests per year within the next 10 years. The successful candidate will be based in Ryanair’s Dublin Offices and report directly to the Senior Medium\-Term Planning Manager. You will work as part of a small team responsible for driving Ryanair’s future growth by developing growth strategies for slot constrained airports. **You Will Be Responsible For** * Manage Ryanair’s historic portfolio of high value take\-off / landing slots. * Growing and improving Slot Portfolio’s at strategic airports. * Manage relationships with national slot coordinators, robustly representing Ryanair’s interests externally when required. * Representing Ryanair at international slot conferences * Construction of Ryanair’s schedule * Integration of new aircraft deliveries into the network * Communication of key scheduling information to senior management in a number of departments. * Work closely with other internal departments to ensure the smooth operation of the schedule, announcement of new routes, etc. * Analysis of schedule efficiency. **Requirements** * Highly organised individual with the ability to balance multiple priorities and hit tight deadlines. * Strong attention to detail and a good problem solver. * Good communication skills, both written and verbal. * Strong communication skills * Fluent in English * Third level qualification an advantage * Systems literate **Benefits** * Competitive salary * Discounted and unlimited travel to over 250 destinations * Defined Contribution Pension Scheme – Matched up to 5% or €5,000 * Death in Service Benefit – Up to 2 times of annual basic salary * 20 Days Annual Leave – Increasing to 22 days after 12 months and 25 days after 3 years of continuous service. * Option for up to 5 additional unpaid leave days per year * Cycle 2 Work Scheme * Unrivalled career progression Competencies Problem Solving/Analysis Adaptability/Flexibility Communication Decision Making/Judgment Job Knowledge Listening Skills Teamwork

title: Operations Associate - Fund Support (Dublin)
description: EMEA Asset Management Dublin Founded in 1848, Lazard operates in 43 cities across 27 countries with two core businesses: asset management and financial advisory. The Lazard Asset Management Group (‘LAM Group’) has circa €198\.5 billion assets under management across a wide range of global, regional and country\-specific strategies \- both traditional and alternative \- in listed equity and fixed income. Lazard Fund Managers (Ireland) Limited (“LFMI”) is a key part of the LAM Group and a physical office was established in 2014\. LFMI’s main business is the provision of fund management and administration services to collective investment schemes. LFMI manages three UCITS (undertakings for collective investment in transferable securities, which were established in accordance with the EU Directive 2009/65/EC). LFMI and the Funds are regulated by the Central Bank of Ireland (the CBI). LFMI has three branch offices for sales in Belgium, Spain and the Netherlands. At Lazard Asset Management, we pride ourselves in balancing the feel of a small firm with the scale of a large global organization. Our entrepreneurial culture and flat management structure foster an environment in which good ideas can take flight, no matter where they come from. **Operations Associate\- Fund Support \- Dublin** To accompany the development of Lazard Asset Management’s UCITS fund ranges, we are seeking an Operations Associate to join our Fund Support sub team. The Fund Support team, based in Dublin, works closely with the sales and marketing teams to provide fund related material, including PRIIPs KIDs, UCITS KIIDs, factsheets, and data internally and externally. **Responsibilities:** * Co\-ordinate the production of monthly factsheets in multiple languages involving: + Sourcing and validating both static and non static data required to populate the factsheets from both internal and external sources. Any source data issues to be investigated and resolved prior to system upload. + Uploading data to the document production system and ensuring the data is populated correctly. + Coordinating checks of the factsheets and signing off once validated. + Arranging publication of the factsheets to both LAM website and 3rd party media platforms + Resolving any queries raised in relation to the final published documents. * Co\-ordinate the production of annual and ad\-hoc both PRIIPs KIDs in multiple languages and UCITS KIIDs. + Sourcing and validating both static and non static data required to populate the PRIIPs KIDs and/or UCITS KIIDs from both internal and external sources. Any source data issues to be investigated and resolved prior to system upload. + Arranging any required translations for PRIIPs KIDs as necessary. + Uploading data to the document production system and ensuring the data is populated correctly. + Coordinating checks of the PRIIPs KIDs/UICTS KIIDs in line with internal procedures, signing off once validated. + Providing the relevant document for filing in the relevant jurisdiction via the internal legal team or external service provider as applicable. + Arranging publication of the documents to LAM website and 3rd party media platforms following receipt of filing confirmation in the relevant jurisdiction. + Resolving any queries raised in relation to the final published documents. * Maintain and manage the relationship with factsheet and KID/KIIDs service * Maintain production logs for the factsheet and KID/ * Liaise with external translators for regular and ad hoc fund documentation * Interface with a range of departments in respect of completion of the documentation, and answer any related queries * Other ad\-hoc duties, g. filing, archiving, sending PDF’s to platforms, answering queries from internal clients * Liaise with subject experts and marketing colleagues across the firm globally to collect and maintain mutual fund relevant information and ensure its accuracy * Ensure data is properly disseminated to data providers and regulators including EMT, EPT, AMT data files. + Sourcing and validating both static and non static data required to populate the data files from both internal and external sources. Any source data issues to be investigated and resolved prior to system upload. + Coordinate production of the file with the relevant service provider and investigate and resolve any data related issues raised during the production process. + Coordinate the dissemination of the data files as necessary and investigate any issues raised by internal or external parties in relation to the files. * Conduct periodic audits of internal and external websites to ensure appropriate documentation is present and updating as required * Maintain appropriate knowledge of the regulations surrounding products in scope * Take a proactive role in launching share classes (obtaining ISINs, tickers, ) * Ensuring the internal product database is up to date at all times to reflect: additional share classes added to Prospectus; newly launched Sub Funds; changes made to existing share classes/Sub Funds. * Complete client templates as required * Any other ad\-hoc tasks as may be required **Essential Skills, Qualifications** * Up to 2 years relevant experience in Fund Administration * We are open to candidates who have relevant internship experience aswell * Comprehensive technical knowledge of UCITS structures across a range of domiciles but predominantly Ireland \& UK * Educated to degree level within a business related discipline **What We Offer** We strive to enhance the total health and well\-being of our employees through comprehensive, competitive benefits. Our goal is to offer a highly individualized employee experience that enables you to balance your commitments to career, family, and community. When you work for Lazard, you are working for an organization that cares about your unique talents and passions, and will continue to invest in the development of your career. **Does this sound like you?** Apply! We’ll get in touch on the next steps. **Inclusion at Lazard** Lazard is an intellectual capital business focused on delivering the best advice and solutions to clients. Achieving these objectives requires us to identify, develop and retain the best talent. A workforce comprised of people with varied backgrounds and experiences creates a rich diversity of thought that empowers us to challenge conventional wisdom, as diverse perspectives lead to better decisions. Our appreciation of diversity’s strength is ingrained in our multicultural heritage. As a global firm that has grown organically from local roots in different countries, we have a deep tradition of respect for individual differences, which has been core to our success for 175 years. The ongoing cultivation of an inclusive culture are essential to our continued growth. We are committed to sustaining an environment in which all employees – regardless of socioeconomic status, race, ethnicity, nationality, religion, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, physical abilities, veteran or military status – can maximize their individual potential, as well as our collective success. \#LAM

title: Pastry Chef
description: **SCOPE \& GENERAL PURPOSE OF JOB:** To be accountable for pastry production areas of the kitchen, working with the team to ensure that Pastries, Breads and Confectionary are prepared and presented to the agreed highest standards. **ACCOUNTABILITIES:** * To offer the highest level of guest service in the Food Production areas of the hotel, ensuring the 4\* agreed standards are achieved at all times. * To ensure that the standard of products consistently meets the expectation of the customers. * Responsible for the production of fresh breads and pastries in accordance with requests from clients, and to provide same for all food service areas * To prepare and present food to a consistently high standard for all food service areas of the hotel. * To follow all requirements as set down by HACCP, and to supervise junior members of the kitchen team in relation to same * To ensure that all hygiene regulations are adhered to at all times and all food production areas are maintained at a consistently high level of cleanliness. * To ensure that all relevant HACCP documentation is completed throughout the working day * To document and report back to supervisors and management any non\-compliance in relation to HACCP control within the kitchen * To comply with statutory and legal requirements for Health \& Safety, Fire, Hygiene, Licensing and Employment. * To have pride and commitment in your area of work. * To attend training sessions as required. * To attend appraisal / assess as required. * To attend Hotel and Departmental Meetings as required, ensuring effective communication at all levels. * To maintain all company policies and procedures. **This is not a full and final list of duties and responsibilities and you may be required to carry out other tasks as directed by senior executives.**

title: People Associate
description: At Flipdish, we’re turning the tables in favour of independent restaurant and takeaway owners by providing them with the tools they need to make their lives easier. We offer a suite of powerful yet straightforward tech solutions along with real human support, helping our customers thrive on their own terms. Everything we do is designed to make running a restaurant satisfyingly simple in a world that’s anything but. **About the role** We’re looking for a People Associate to join our People team. This is a hands\-on role with exposure across the full employee life cycle, working closely with a small but mighty People team. You will support day\-to\-day People operations while also getting involved in broader People initiatives, projects and improvements. This role is ideal for someone early in their People career who wants to make an impact across a growing scale\-up, take on real responsibility, and loves variety **What you will be doing** * Supporting the full employee life cycle from onboarding to offboarding * Managing People Operations tasks including contracts, documentation, system updates and employee records * Coordinating onboarding and induction for new hires * Supporting performance review cycles and career development processes * Assisting with employee relations queries and escalating where appropriate * Maintaining accuracy across People systems and reports * Supporting compliance and policy administration * Getting involved in wider People initiatives such as engagement, wellbeing, learning and development or process improvements * Working closely with managers and other teams to get things done efficiently **What we are looking for** * Early experience in a People, HR or People Operations role, or a strong interest in building a career in this area * Excellent attention to detail and a high standard of accuracy * Strong organisational skills and the ability to manage multiple tasks at once * A proactive, hands\-on approach with a focus on getting things done * Comfortable working in a fast\-paced, scaling environment * Strong communication skills and a professional, people\-first approach * Discretion and the ability to handle confidential information appropriately **What you will get** * Broad exposure across People Operations in a scaling SaaS environment * Hands\-on experience rather than narrow admin\-only work * Support, mentoring and a clear development pathway within the People function * The opportunity to make an impact and help shape how we work as a company 🦄 **About Flipdish** We are Flipdish, an Irish unicorn valued at over $1bn, helping independent restaurants, takeaways and quick service brands compete and grow in a world transformed by technology. Our platform has evolved into a fully integrated, AI powered ecosystem that supports restaurants with online ordering, marketing, loyalty, point of sale, menu optimisation and operational automation. AI is at the heart of many of our newest innovations, including the Flipdish AI Phone Agent, which answers customer calls, takes orders and ensures no order is ever missed. From AI driven marketing to intelligent menu tools, we build technology that makes running a restaurant satisfyingly simple in a world that is anything but. But Flipdish is not just about technology. We are deeply connected to the industry we serve. Many of us have worked in restaurants, run franchises or grown food businesses ourselves. We understand the pressures operators face, and we support them with the same intensity and care we would want for our own businesses. **🫀Our culture** At this point, you might expect the usual spiel about how great a place Flipdish is to work at and how great our work culture is (and it *is* !). But, this all depends on our employees. And that’s because we trust our employees to define what sort of an employer Flipdish is. Transparency is hugely important to us. We listen to one another. We collaborate. And we give everyone a way to grow their careers through a supportive environment. We’re a company that values diversity, inclusion, and wellbeing as standard. We believe in balance, not burnout. And all permanent employees qualify for our share scheme because we share our success. We certainly encourage flexibility where possible and combine this with beautiful offices in bustling cities like Dublin, London and Karachi. We’re focused on getting the most out of our time together, output and team energy is at the heart of our hub based philosophy \- not an attendance ticking exercise! So, if you want to join a genuinely friendly team, take a look at our current openings and get in touch. And if you don’t see an opening just yet, do get in touch and register your interest with us anyway. We’ll be in touch as soon as something suitable opens up. **🙈Don’t be shy. Apply.** We know that imposter syndrome can be a thing. Don’t let it be. We want enthusiastic people from all backgrounds. If you feel like you’re up for working with us, get in touch. We also understand some candidates might need assistance. That’s not a problem in the slightest. Just reach out to us, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Person in Charge Social Care Dunshane Co Kildare
description: **Camphill Communities of Ireland (CCoI) are recruiting for:** **Person in Charge** Camphill Communities of Ireland (CCoI) supports people with physical/intellectual disabilities and autism. CCoI operates Residential and Day Services across 15 locations, in which residents and day\-attendees are supported to live an ordinary life, a life like any other. We are currently recruiting for a **Person in Charge** to lead our Residential Service in **Dunshane nr Naas Co. Kildare.** **Job Title:** Person in Charge **Location:** Naas Co Kildare **Contract:** Permanent **Hours:** Fulltime \- 40 hours per week **Salary:** €54,953 \- €65,807 per annum **(plus on call allowance of €3,904 per annum)** As Person in Charge, you will have the opportunity to work as part of a committed team, we offer a competitive salary, and you will have access to collaborative and learning work within a leading national organisation. **What you will need:** * A minimum of a Level 7 on the QQI Framework \- BA in Social Care Studies, Social Care Management or equivalent relevant qualification in Health or Social Care. * Post graduate qualification ideally in Social Care Management or Management Qualification. * At least 3 years of senior management experience at a similar level of responsibility including demonstrative experience strategic planning. * At least 5 years' experience in the health/social care or voluntary sector. * Possess the competencies and skills appropriate to working with persons with an intellectual disability. **Please note all posts are subject to Garda Vetting, relevant Police clearance for any country of residence of over 6 months from the age of 18 and reference checking** **Camphill Communities of Ireland is an equal opportunities employer**

title: Pharmacy Technician
description: **Care Home Pharmacy Technician required to join our rapidly expanding team .** **Location:** Cara Allcare Pharmacy, Pottery Road, Dún Laoghaire, Co. Dublin **Role Overview:** Our Carehome pharmacy business continues to grow from our base in Pottery road DunLaoghaire Co. Dublin . Would you like to be part of a young dynamic team ? We are currently seeking several dedicated and detail\-oriented **Care Home Pharmacy Technicians** to join our team at Cara Allcare Pharmacy. The successful candidates will play a key role in managing care home accounts, ensuring efficient and accurate medication preparation and delivery, and maintaining strong professional relationships with care home staff and healthcare professionals. **Key Responsibilities:** * Build and maintain strong relationships with nurses, care workers, and GPs associated with assigned care homes. * Process all daily orders for designated accounts in a timely and accurate manner, ready for pharmacist verification. * Ensure monthly orders are collected, processed, and completed before the specified deadlines. * Follow up on any queries with GP practices promptly and professionally. * Adhere to company Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) at all times. * Strive to enhance customer satisfaction and deliver a consistently high standard of service to all care home accounts. * Work collaboratively within a team environment and provide support to colleagues when needed. * Communicate effectively with the Technician Manager regarding any issues or feedback from care homes. * Demonstrate a high level of attention to detail and accountability in all areas of work. **Essential Criteria:** * A recognised Pharmacy Technician or Dispensing Technician qualification, or extensive relevant experience in a similar role. * Minimum of **2 years’ experience** working as a pharmacy technician in a busy pharmacy setting. * Proficiency with **McLernons Dispensing System** is essential. * Strong data entry, organisational, and time\-management skills. * Fluent in English with excellent written and verbal communication abilities. * A flexible and proactive attitude with the ability to work independently and as part of a team. * High level of accuracy and attention to detail. * Willingness to undergo full care home training provided by the pharmacy. * Ability to confidently present information to patients and small groups, and collaborate with other healthcare professionals. **Desirable:** * Previous experience working with care homes is an advantage, though not essential. **Cara Allcare Pharmacy** is committed to providing exceptional service and patient care. If you’re passionate about healthcare, great customer service are detail\-oriented, and thrive in a collaborative team environment, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Piping Engineer
description: **About Tate** At Tate we are passionate about everything we do. As an independent brand operating within Kingspan Group, a global plc, Tate has been recognised worldwide as an industry leader in the development and manufacture of data centre infrastructure solutions and commercial office raised access floors for over 60 years. With revenues of over $500m and growing, Tate plays a pivotal role in offering expertise in cutting edge design engineering, working collaboratively with clients as a trusted partner. As a market leader within our industry Tate continues to grow and expand, operating 12 manufacturing sites across the US, Europe, and Australia. We are excited about our fresh, dynamic, and inclusive team of experts working on new innovations and forward\-thinking designs. We continue to invest heavily in the best available manufacturing tools and equipment needed to adhere to Tate's world class standards. In keeping with our Planet Passionate sustainability strategy, our mission is to accelerate a net zero emissions future built environment with the wellbeing of people and planet at its heart. **About the Role** The Piping Engineer will lead the engineering of integrated piping systems, ensuring compliance with industry standards, and project specifications for data centre liquid cooling applications. You will work closely with the business development team to identify new opportunities, develop conceptual designs and drawings to be presented to potential customers. They will also work very closely with the Manufacturing team to ensure designs are optimal, practical, energy and cost efficient. **Key Responsibilities** **Piping Design and Layout**: Develop detailed piping designs, including 3D models, pdf drawings, and piping layouts for Data Centre projects **Stress Analysis**: Perform piping stress analysis ensure system integrity under various operating conditions. **Material Selection**: Specify piping materials, components, and fittings based on project requirements **Compliance and Standards**: Ensure designs adhere to industry and client specifications. **Project Coordination**: Collaborate with project managers, commercial team, and other stakeholders to integrate piping systems with overall project designs. **Technical Leadership**: Mentor and guide junior engineers, review their work, and provide technical direction. **Documentation**: Prepare and review piping specifications, bill of materials (BOM), and technical reports. **Site Support**: Provide technical support during construction, commissioning, and troubleshooting of piping systems. **Cost Management:** Contribute to project cost estimation and ensure designs are completed within budget and timeline. **Qualifications, Experience \& Skills** **Education:** HNC / Degree / Post Graduate Degree Qualifications in Mechanical / Building Services Engineering. **Experience:** 5\-7 years of industry experience in piping design and engineering in pharmaceuticals, food processing, oil \& gas, wastewater, chemical, construction, or semiconductor industries. Exposure to Data Centres or Critical environments is highly desirable. **Skills** * Good working knowledge of manufacturing processes, materials, and quality control protocols. * Proficiency in 3D modelling and piping design software (e.g. Solidworks, Revit, AutoCAD, PDMS, SP3D, CADWorx). * Expertise in stress analysis tools * Knowledge of industry codes and standards * Experience developing new products and taking them from concept to prototype to production. * Strong communication and teamwork skills. * Excellent time management and organisational skills, with the ability to set objectives and prioritise workloads for yourself. * Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment and interface with sales, customer service, purchasing, manufacturing, engineering, quality and marketing. Stay connected with us on *LinkedIn* for insights into life at Tate. Tate is an equal\-opportunity employer. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences. Join us in our mission to make a difference through exceptional solutions.

title: Project Engineer - Water Networks
description: **The Opportunity** Are you a talented **Project Engineer** with a passion for water network design? We have an exciting opportunity for a highly skilled and motivated individual to join our team at Ryan Hanley, now part of Stantec. You'll play a key role in the planning, design, and delivery of crucial water network projects, helping to shape the future of Ireland's water infrastructure. Your responsibilities will include developing detailed designs for water network infrastructure, including pumping stations and storage facilities. You will also prepare feasibility studies, site assessments, and technical evaluations, as well as create specifications and tender documents for projects. You will coordinate with clients, contractors, and our multidisciplinary teams, and you'll manage and guide others in preparing project drawings and other inputs. Throughout the entire project lifecycle, you will provide expert technical support. **About You** You will have a passion for the water sector and have the following : * A third\-level degree in engineering (Level 8 minimum). * Strong post graduate experience in water network design. * Strong professional and technical knowledge. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * The ability to work independently and as part of a collaborative team. * A keen eye for detail and strong problem\-solving skills. * The ability to manage deadlines and juggle multiple tasks effectively. * Proficiency in industry\-standard software. **Why Join Us?** * Competitive salary and a comprehensive benefits package. * Access to excellent professional growth and development opportunities. * A collaborative and supportive work environment. * Flexibility to be based in our Dublin, Galway, Cork, or Castlebar office. * Work on impactful projects that are making a real difference across Ireland. If you're ready to take on a challenging and rewarding role, we'd love to hear from you. **Interested? Apply now to join our dynamic team and help us build a better future.** **About Stantec** Whether you’re starting out on your engineering career or are looking for a fresh challenge, Ryan Hanley, now part of Stantec, is a great career move. Based in Dublin, Galway, Castlebar and Cork, we are actively recruiting for team players with experience in any of the following areas – water and wastewater treatment process and network design, integrated network management, structural design, site development, road and bridge engineering, project management and administration. You’ll be working for a dynamic company with an excellent reputation in the industry. Ryan Hanley, now part of Stantec, offers a great working environment amongst friendly professionals with an emphasis on teamwork. It’s a company where your ideas and opinions will be valued and your efforts recognised and rewarded. We have been awarded the silver Investors in Diversity accreditation by the Irish Centre for Diversity for our commitment to quality, diversity and inclusion (EDI) in the workplace.

title: Project Quantity Surveyor
company: GEDON™ | Commercial & Executive Search for the Built Environment
description: **ABOUT THE BUSINESS** Our client is a well\-established specialist subcontractor operating across the glazing and façade sector, with a strong presence in the Irish construction market. With a portfolio that spans bespoke residential developments, large\-scale mixed\-use commercial schemes and complex healthcare facilities, this is a business that consistently delivers technically challenging work to the highest standard. Having built a strong reputation for quality and reliability over many years, the company has developed long\-standing relationships with leading main contractors, developers and end clients across Ireland. Their ability to manage technically intricate packages — from design coordination through to installation — has made them a trusted name across multiple sectors. The business is in a strong period of growth, supported by an active pipeline of projects and a robust order book. As a result, there is a deliberate focus on strengthening the commercial team to support continued delivery and position the business well for future opportunities. This is an excellent time to join a company with clear ambition and the infrastructure to support professional development. **WORKING ENVIRONMENT \& PROJECT EXPOSURE** You will be based in the company's Dublin office, working alongside an experienced commercial and project delivery team. The business operates with a dedicated site team who manage all on\-site activities, meaning your focus will be on the commercial and contractual management of projects from the office environment — providing a structured and professional working setting. The projects you will work across are varied and technically interesting, ranging from bespoke high\-end residential schemes through to large\-scale mixed\-use commercial developments and healthcare facilities. Contract values typically run up to €5 million per package, offering the opportunity to manage meaningful projects with real commercial substance at each stage of delivery. The team culture is collaborative and professional, with experienced senior staff available to provide guidance and mentorship. You will gain strong exposure to the full commercial lifecycle of specialist subcontract packages, working closely with estimating, project management and procurement functions throughout the process. **KEY RESPONSIBILITIES** * Manage the full commercial lifecycle of specialist façade and glazing packages from award through to final account * Prepare and submit interim valuations, variations and claims in line with contract requirements * Identify, value and negotiate contract variations and ensure accurate change management records are maintained * Liaise with main contractors and clients on all commercial and contractual matters throughout the project * Monitor project costs and budgets, providing regular commercial reporting to the senior team * Support procurement activities including package letting and subcontractor management * Assist in the preparation of final accounts and ensure timely resolution of outstanding commercial matters **WHAT YOU NEED** * A degree in Quantity Surveying or a closely related construction discipline is essential * 2–4 years of post\-graduate commercial experience within the construction or specialist subcontract sector * Previous experience working for a subcontractor or specialist trade contractor is desirable * A solid understanding of contract administration, variations and valuation processes * Strong communication and negotiation skills with the ability to manage client and contractor relationships professionally * Well\-organised, commercially aware and able to manage multiple projects simultaneously * Proficiency in standard QS software and Microsoft Office suite **WHAT YOU'LL GET IN RETURN** This role offers a salary of up to €80,000 depending on experience, alongside a performance\-related bonus, company pension and a flexible work\-from\-home arrangement available following a 12\-month review period. You will be joining a business that is actively growing its Dublin operation, with a clear opportunity to progress your career within a strong and well\-resourced commercial team. The variety of project types on offer — across residential, commercial and healthcare — ensures a genuinely well\-rounded experience and a platform to develop your expertise at pace. **HOW TO APPLY** To apply for this role, please send an up\-to\-date CV via the Apply Now button. For a confidential discussion, you can also contact **Finn Davey** on **\+353 1 270 9457** or email **finn@gedonexecutive.com** .

title: QS Cost Specialist
description: Vacancy Number: 27674 Category: Quantity Surveyor Job Type: Full\-time **The Networks \& Civil, Environmental Engineering Department (NCEE)** within Engineering and Major Projects provides a range of planning, design, construction, operation \& maintenance, and management services required for electricity industry infrastructure projects in Ireland. **Senior Quantity Surveyor** \- **The Cost Specialist** role will encompass all aspects of quantity surveying including cost, contract and commercial risk management for our full range of civil engineering projects related to Networks infrastructure development in ESB. The role will encompass close liaison with other design professionals, our partners across ESB and contractors. Increasing visibility of the Quantity Surveying function across ESB, while maintaining standards, developing staff and enhancing stakeholder management are key requirement for this role. **Key Responsibilities** * Provide knowledge and expert advice to internal and external stakeholders on commercial matters and risk identification on projects delivered by both traditional re\-measurable contracts and other contracting models such as D\&B and EPC. * Provide strong leadership skills in the development of commercial management capability, through integration of commercial lessons learned, mentoring of junior staff and enhanced collaboration. * Drive a culture of continuous improvement on commercial contract issues and cost management through developing/updating procedures and knowledge transfer to colleagues. * Deliver full range of commercial management services for the project lifecycle, to our Networks business on projects of strategic importance. * Be accountable for the delivery of the commercial tender enquiry documentation including use of ARM 5 for BOQ preparation, and subsequent commercial assessment and critical evaluation of tenders, including collaborating with the various procurement functions across ESB, for large scale projects. * Be accountable for cost monitoring of the civil works and/or other elements on projects/contracts throughout the project lifecycle and advise on the cost accrual v Capex Approval. * Provide strong negotiating skills, to facilitate determination of complex commercial matters or disputes, for contract awards, claim assessments and final account agreements, which deliver value for our Stakeholders. **Experience and Qualifications** **Essential** * Minimum of seven years post\-graduate experience. * Strong knowledge of the administration of the main conditions of contract including the FIDIC Conditions of Contract. * Detailed knowledge in Cost estimating, Tendering, Post Tender evaluation and assessment, and Pre\-contract negotiation. * Detailed knowledge in Cost Control reporting and management. * A flexible approach to work, especially where necessary to deliver excellent Stakeholder Management service. * Self\-motivation and ability to project\-manage own work and coordinate with those working alongside. * Ability to work and share knowledge and competencies to develop junior staff. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * Full drivers’ licence valid for use in Republic of Ireland and have own car **Desirable** * Experience in the use of Cubit software would be advantageous. * Experience in Civil Engineering / Utility project contracts in excess of €10m in contract value would be advantageous. * Chartered status in a recognised professional institution. * Recent Experience in managing a Commercial team. **Qualifications** **Essential** * Level 8 Degree in Quantity Surveying or equivalent work experience. **Location** This role is based in EMP Dublin or Cork office but travel to project locations as required, is a key requirement of the role. ESB is committed to offering flexibility in ways of working and we call that Smart Working – combining digital tools with a culture of trust and empowerment to enable flexible and remote ways of working. The successful candidate may combine working from home within the Republic of Ireland, with attendance at base for in\-person collaboration, the frequency of which is agreed with the manager and may change as we learn about hybrid working. Site visits is also a feature of this role which will require a certain amount of travel within Ireland. **Reporting To** Pre\-CA and Construction Manager in Networks Civil \& Environmental Engineering **Why Work with Us?** * Opportunity to lead Ireland’s transition to net zero carbon future * Career development through mentoring and training * Corporate Social Responsibility Opportunities * Sports and Social Clubs * Networking opportunities * Credit Union * Generous Pension * Access to staff well\-being programmes * Generous parental leave entitlements * Strong values\-based and inclusive culture * Strong commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion * Great team environment working to our Core Values: Courageous, Caring, Driven and Trusted **Salary** €71,000 \- €84,000 Per Annum **Closing Date** 1st May 2026 **Notes:** Your application will be held in reserve for 6 months should you be suitable for the role. The successful candidate will be required to evidence their right to work in the \<\<\<\< Republic of Ireland / UK \>\>\>\> prior to commencement of employment. **Diversity, Equity and Inclusion Statement** ESB is committed to being an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all sections of society and ensure that no one is discriminated against on the grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnicity or nationality, disability, age, citizenship, marital status, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation or gender identity, or any other basis as protected by law. Applicants who anticipate requiring assistance or reasonable accommodations for any part of the application or interview process may contact, in confidence, recruitment@esb.ie. **About ESB** Join us in our mission to achieve a net\-zero electricity system by 2040\. ESB is Ireland’s leading energy utility, with activities spanning electricity generation, transmission and distribution, energy supply, energy services and international consultancy. Operating in Ireland, Northern Ireland and Great Britain, we invest over €1 billion each year to deliver a new energy future based on reliable, affordable zero\-carbon electricity.
