title: Surveyor
description: **Job Title** Surveyor **Job Description Summary** **Job Description** Surveyor Dublin Cushman \& Wakefield are now seeking a motivated Surveyor to join our high‑performing Dublin team. This is an excellent opportunity for an MRICS‑qualified professional looking to further develop their career within a dynamic, collaborative, and growth‑driven environment. **Key Responsibilities** * Undertake valuation instructions across a wide mix of asset classes for loan security, financial reporting, acquisition, and strategic purposes. * Deliver high‑quality valuation reports and advice to a broad range of clients. * Manage day‑to‑day execution of instructions with appropriate supervision. * Contribute to building and maintaining strong client relationships. * Support in the delivery of regular valuation programmes and complex one‑off assignments. * Help promote the Valuation \& Advisory service line to existing and prospective clients. * Play an active role in supporting the development of more junior colleagues. **Skills \& Competencies** * Strong analytical and numerical skills with the ability to interpret market trends. * Ability to form well‑reasoned opinions on property values. * Confident managing multiple projects and working to deadlines. * Proficient in Excel, Word and PowerPoint. * Strong communication and relationship‑building skills. * Creative thinker able to approach challenges with a solutions‑focused mindset. **Knowledge \& Experience** * MRICS qualified. * Familiarity with valuation software (e.g., Argus) is desirable. * 1–3 years’ post‑qualification experience within commercial valuation (Irish market exposure preferred but not essential). * Experience working with institutional clients, lenders or property companies is advantageous. **Why Join Us?** * Opportunity to work with one of Ireland’s most respected valuation teams. * Exposure to high‑profile assets and leading market participants. * Career progression within a global organisation committed to professional development. * A collaborative, supportive, and forward‑thinking team environment. INCO: “Cushman \& Wakefield”

title: Tax Lawyer
description: A\& Goodbody's Tax team is seeking to hire an experienced qualified Tax Lawyer and we would welcome expressions of interest from Tax Lawyers with **2 \- 5 years PQE** for the Dublin team. The Tax team at A\&L Goodbody works with many of the world's leading corporates and financial institutions providing a broad spectrum of Irish taxation advisory and transactional services. **Requirements** **Key duties and responsibilities** Candidates will have the opportunity to be involved across a range of areas and industry sectors; * International tax and cross border planning for corporate reorganisations, restructurings and inward investment projects into Ireland * Mergers, acquisitions, demergers and IPOs * Tax controversy * Capital markets and structured finance transactions * Private equity transactions * Transfer pricing * Asset and project finance for international and domestic aircraft and equipment * Real estate tax * The ideal candidate will have 2 \- 5 years relevant PQE within a corporate law firm * Candidates must be a qualified lawyer and have a professional tax / accounting qualification **Benefits** * A competitive remuneration package * Access to the firm's marquee training and development programmes, delivered through the ALG School of Business and Law * Discretionary performance bonus * Pension and health insurance contribution, wellbeing contribution, life assurance, complimentary conveyancing allowance, subsidised gym, restaurant and coffee shop * Hybrid working policy **The Firm** A\&L Goodbody (ALG) is a leading Irish law firm with five international offices in Dublin, Belfast, London, New York and San Francisco. There are currently 120 partners and over 900 employees. The strong international and domestic business community in Ireland forms a valuable part of the ALG client base. ALG is incredibly proud to be recognised as the leading Irish law firm in the Chambers and Partners Europe 2024 Guide with the highest number of Band 1 practice area rankings. ALG is also ranked as a 'top tier' law firm in The Legal 500 EMEA Guide 2024 and the IFLR1000 Guide 2024\. The firm is consistently ranked number one in the Irish market and on a European level. A sample of other recent notable awards include: * Awarded the prestigious Company of the Year award at the National Diversity \& Inclusion Awards 2025 hosted by the Irish Centre for Diversity * Recognised by Mergermarket as the \#1 M\&A legal advisor in Ireland by deal volume for 2024 * Winner of the Best Client Service Award by the Lawyer European Awards * Future of Legal Services Innovation Award winners 2024 * Awarded the Investors in Diversity Silver Mark in 2024 by the Irish Centre for Diversity * Most Popular Graduate Recruiter in Law 2024 by GradIreland (for 13 consecutive years) * Law firm of the year \- Republic of Ireland 2023 at The Lawyer European Awards * Irish Law Firm of the Year 2023 at the Chambers Europe Awards * One of the top 50 most innovative law firms in Europe 2023 by the Financial Times * Best client support 2023 by the Managing Partners Forum * Business Working Responsibly Mark 2023 ALG has a reputation for leading the way in continuously developing its service to clients and internally to its business. This is delivered through the use of new processes, ways of working, innovative use of technology and marketing\-leading business service teams.

title: Technical Business Analyst
description: **Technical Business Analyst – Greenfield Opportunity** ● Own delivery from requirements through to implementation ● Work closely with customers and engineering teams ● Shape data structure, reporting, and visualisation Join a high\-growth SaaS company transforming how organisations use data to make decisions. Their platform replaces manual reporting with a centralised, real\-time solution that drives smarter business outcomes. They’re hiring a Technical Business Analyst to deliver customer\-facing solutions in a greenfield environment. You’ll engage directly with clients, understand their reporting challenges, and translate requirements into clear user stories within Azure DevOps. You’ll take ownership of data mapping, calculations, and dashboard logic—ensuring accurate, scalable delivery. This is a high\-impact role at the intersection of customers, data, and technology, offering strong ownership in a fast\-paced SaaS environment. **What you’ll be doing:** * Lead customer implementations from discovery through to delivery * Work directly with clients to understand reporting needs and challenges * Translate requirements into clear user stories within Azure DevOps * Define data mapping, calculations, and dashboard logic * Own and prioritise the implementation backlog * Ensure solutions are delivered accurately, efficiently, and at scale **What they’re looking for:** * 1–2 years’ experience as a Technical Business Analyst * Experience with software implementations * Experience working on data migration, reporting, or analytics projects * Strong customer\-facing and stakeholder management skills * Experience with Azure DevOps, Jira, or similar tools * Excellent requirements gathering and documentation skills * Exposure to SQL, data modelling, or data structures is a plus **Package:** €45,000 – €50,000 \+ Bonus \+ Annual Leave \+ Health Insurance For more information, contact Brandon Foley on 01 649 8508 or brandon.foley@archer.ie

title: Technical Business Analyst – 6- Month Contract
description: **Technical Business Analyst – 6\- Month Contract, South Dublin** Don’t miss out on this great opportunity. Apply today for an immediate start!!! **Technical Business Analyst Role and Responsibilities** : Our client is seeking a Technical Business Analyst to support the enhancement of Finance and IT control environments. This role is key to strengthening governance, improving risk visibility, and supporting compliance. You will work across critical initiatives including Privileged Access Monitoring (PAM), Segregation of Duties (SoD), and Log Gap Analysis—partnering with technology, risk, and business teams to deliver control improvements. **What you’ll do:** * Gather and document business and technical requirements across IT control initiatives * Analyse privileged access, user roles, and system logs to identify control gaps and risk exposure * Conduct Segregation of Duties (SoD) assessments and support remediation planning * Perform log gap analysis across systems to ensure adequate monitoring and compliance * Translate complex technical findings into clear, actionable insights and documentation * Facilitate workshops with stakeholders across Technology, Risk, and Business teams * Support audit and regulatory requirements through high\-quality reporting and evidence **What you need:** * Experience in technology risk, IT controls, security, or governance programmes * Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret access models, logs, and technical data * Experience working with datasets (Excel, SQL, or similar tools) * Understanding of privileged access, IAM, SIEM, or logging frameworks * Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills Desirable skills * Experience with IAM/PAM tools (e.g., CyberArk) * Knowledge of SoD frameworks or SOX controls * Exposure to cloud platforms and logging/monitoring tools **What’s on offer:** * Competitive daily rate depending on experience * Hybrid working model\- 2 days on site * High chance of contract renewal **Apply Now** If you would like to discuss this opportunity in person or with one of our IT Resourcers please forward your CV to Vantage Resources or contact Eric Seery on eric.seery@vantage.ie for a confidential discussion. Vantage Resources will not forward your details without prior discussion and approval. Vantage Resources is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive equal consideration for engagement and/or employment. An inclusive and diverse workforce is an essential part of the development of our organisation’s culture which we believe enhances both our working environment and the service we provide to our customers.

title: Technical Solutions Engineer Associate, Scaled Solutions
description: Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: **Eemshaven, Netherlands; Saint\-Ghislain, Belgium; Fredericia, Denmark; Hamina, Finland; Dublin, Ireland** . **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor’s degree in Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics, or equivalent practical experience. * 2 years of experience troubleshooting and advocating for customers' needs, and triaging technical issues (e.g., hardware, software, application, operational, process). * Experience reading code in a general purpose coding language (e.g., Java, C, C\+\+, Python, Shell, Go, JavaScript) or in system design. **Preferred qualifications:** * 2 years of experience with 2 or more of the following: Web Technology, Data/Big Data, Systems Administration, Machine Learning, Networking, Kubernetes, Oracle, SAP. * Experience in technical consulting on supporting enterprise customers with high service\-level objective and service\-level agreement requirements. * Experience with cloud computing (i.e., certifications, internships, coursework, etc.). * Experience owning a full life\-cycle of technical projects including design, development, maintenance, and driving long term improvements. * Experience developing backend applications or shell scripts. **About the job** The Google Cloud Platform team helps customers transform and build what's next for their business — all with technology built in the cloud. Our products are developed for security, reliability and scalability, running the full stack from infrastructure to applications to devices and hardware. Our teams are dedicated to helping our customers — developers, small and large businesses, educational institutions and government agencies — see the benefits of our technology come to life. As part of an entrepreneurial team in this rapidly growing business, you will play a key role in understanding the needs of our customers and help shape the future of businesses of all sizes use technology to connect with customers, employees and partners. As a part of the Scaled Solutions team, you will work with Google Server Operations and the Data Center Software teams, delivering technical and process solutions that drive operational excellence. In this role, you will help problem\-solve at scale, troubleshooting issues globally by creating innovations in technology, operational processes, and policies. You will focus on resolving workflow execution blockers due to systems and software issues that arise across the supply chain software stack in the course of capacity fulfillment and enablement operations in Google data centers around the world by delivering innovative solutions and drive operational efficiency and cost\-savings for Google Cloud. You will have a mature set of performance metrics and invest in a culture of continuous improvement and operational excellence, having a strong technical background is an important component of the role as we support multiple backend systems with interdependencies. **Responsibilities** * Diagnose and troubleshoot data center blockers and stakeholder concerns to achieve swift resolution. * Design, improve, and iterate on internal tools, software, and automated frameworks to streamline the issue management life\-cycle. * Create standardized technical playbooks and processes that enable the Google Server Operations team to manage and resolve issues autonomously. * Partner with Engineering and Server Operations stakeholders to investigate, identify, and fix the fundamental root causes of negative business outcomes. * Manage project schedules and timelines while fostering engagement with various cross\-functional partners and providing essential business support by participating in weekend on\-call duties or assigned work shift rotations. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: Technical Solutions Engineer Associate, Scaled Solutions
description: Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: **Eemshaven, Netherlands; Saint\-Ghislain, Belgium; Fredericia, Denmark; Hamina, Finland; Dublin, Ireland**.### **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor’s degree in Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics, or equivalent practical experience. * 2 years of experience troubleshooting and advocating for customers' needs, and triaging technical issues (e.g., hardware, software, application, operational, process). * Experience reading code in a general purpose coding language (e.g., Java, C, C\+\+, Python, Shell, Go, JavaScript) or in system design. ### **Preferred qualifications:** * 2 years of experience with 2 or more of the following: Web Technology, Data/Big Data, Systems Administration, Machine Learning, Networking, Kubernetes, Oracle, SAP. * Experience in technical consulting on supporting enterprise customers with high service\-level objective and service\-level agreement requirements. * Experience with cloud computing (i.e., certifications, internships, coursework, etc.). * Experience owning a full life\-cycle of technical projects including design, development, maintenance, and driving long term improvements. * Experience developing backend applications or shell scripts. **About the job** ----------------- The Google Cloud Platform team helps customers transform and build what's next for their business — all with technology built in the cloud. Our products are developed for security, reliability and scalability, running the full stack from infrastructure to applications to devices and hardware. Our teams are dedicated to helping our customers — developers, small and large businesses, educational institutions and government agencies — see the benefits of our technology come to life. As part of an entrepreneurial team in this rapidly growing business, you will play a key role in understanding the needs of our customers and help shape the future of businesses of all sizes use technology to connect with customers, employees and partners. As a part of the Scaled Solutions team, you will work with Google Server Operations and the Data Center Software teams, delivering technical and process solutions that drive operational excellence. In this role, you will help problem\-solve at scale, troubleshooting issues globally by creating innovations in technology, operational processes, and policies. You will focus on resolving workflow execution blockers due to systems and software issues that arise across the supply chain software stack in the course of capacity fulfillment and enablement operations in Google data centers around the world by delivering innovative solutions and drive operational efficiency and cost\-savings for Google Cloud. You will have a mature set of performance metrics and invest in a culture of continuous improvement and operational excellence, having a strong technical background is an important component of the role as we support multiple backend systems with interdependencies.**Responsibilities** -------------------- * Diagnose and troubleshoot data center blockers and stakeholder concerns to achieve swift resolution. * Design, improve, and iterate on internal tools, software, and automated frameworks to streamline the issue management life\-cycle. * Create standardized technical playbooks and processes that enable the Google Server Operations team to manage and resolve issues autonomously. * Partner with Engineering and Server Operations stakeholders to investigate, identify, and fix the fundamental root causes of negative business outcomes. * Manage project schedules and timelines while fostering engagement with various cross\-functional partners and providing essential business support by participating in weekend on\-call duties or assigned work shift rotations. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form.

title: Technical Talent Acquisition Specialist
description: **Our Purpose** *Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200\+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.* **Title and Summary** Technical Talent Acquisition Specialist We have an exciting opportunity for a Technical Talent Acquisition Specialist to help us scale our technology teams across Europe. This is a strategic role where you’ll partner with senior leaders to attract and hire exceptional technical talent, driving innovation and growth in areas such as Software Engineering, DevOps, Agile Delivery, Open Banking, AI, and Machine Learning along with Technical PM \& Product You’ll join a high\-performing team with a strong focus on direct sourcing, delivering a world\-class candidate experience, and shaping the future of tech hiring for our European Tech HQ and regional offices. What You’ll Do Talent Strategy \& Advisory Partner with senior stakeholders to define hiring needs and workforce plans. Provide market insights on tech talent trends, compensation, and competitor activity. Candidate Sourcing \& Engagement Design and execute innovative sourcing strategies for niche technical roles. Build and nurture talent pipelines through proactive engagement in tech communities. Recruitment Process Management Own end\-to\-end recruitment for senior and specialized technical positions. Deliver an exceptional candidate experience from first contact to onboarding. Data \& Insights Use analytics to optimize recruitment processes and report on key metrics. Drive continuous improvement through data\-driven recommendations. What We’re Looking For Technical Knowledge: Strong understanding of software engineering, cloud, data, and emerging technologies. Recruitment Expertise: Proven success in sourcing and closing senior technical talent. Stakeholder Management: Ability to influence and build relationships at all levels. Tools \& Tech: Proficiency with Workday ATS, CRM, LinkedIn Recruiter, and sourcing platforms. Analytical Skills: Experience using data to drive decisions and improve processes. Communication: Exceptional verbal and written communication skills. Why Join Us? Be part of a high\-impact team shaping the future of technology hiring across Europe. Work on cutting\-edge roles in AI, ML, and Open Banking. Enjoy a culture that values inclusion, innovation, and career growth. Competitive compensation, benefits, and opportunities for development. Ready to make an impact? Apply now and help us build the next generation of tech talent!**Corporate Security Responsibility** All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must: * Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices; * Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed; * Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and * Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

title: Test Analyst
description: #### **Company Description** The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland (RCPI), with its Faculties and Institutes, provides postgraduate medical training and professional development opportunities to doctors working in 25 specialties. Collectively, we are the largest provider of postgraduate medical training in Ireland. Our mission is to ensure that patients receive the best possible care. We do this by training and assessing doctors and providing accredited education and professional development programmes. We also quality‑assure standards in specialist medical practice and promote good health through our policy and public outreach initiatives. Our members and senior management team are heavily involved in the current transformation of the Irish health service. We work closely with the Medical Council of Ireland, the Health Service Executive, the Department of Health, and other postgraduate medical training bodies. We are also undergoing significant internal changes. We are implementing a major programme of transformation that has seen the creation of new business initiatives, the internationalisation of the organisation and closer collaboration with key policy leaders in healthcare. We are continuing to adopt a more proactive and progressive role in the Irish and international health sector and have become a more outward‑looking organisation in recent years as a result. As new initiatives have been developed, our workforce has grown, and we now employ approximately 180 staff. We value creativity and innovation and encourage our staff to ‘think outside the box’. Our staff surveys consistently show that the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland is a great place to work, with a friendly, supportive atmosphere and a sense that everyone is playing their part in improving Irish healthcare. RCPI’s vision is “A globally connected community of doctors improving people’s health”, and RCPI’s mission is to develop and support our learners, trainees, members and fellows to be leaders in healthcare delivery and transformation through training, education and advocacy. **The Connect Programme** The “Connect Programme” is a large transformational initiative that will enable people, processes, technology and organisational structures to develop capabilities to meet our mission. The programme is driven by a sense of urgency, is broad in scope and impact, and will be delivered in parallel with ongoing operations. The proposed change initiative and software solution upgrade aim to enable the transformation of the College. This transformation will be enabled by changes in process and structure to critical College capabilities, including business development and recruitment, admissions/applications, registry and record management, training/education delivery and services delivery. These transformation changes will allow the College to enhance communication with prospective trainees, learners, members and fellows (TLMF) and applicants, and improve overall administrative efficiency. This strategic initiative seeks to establish RCPI as a leading institution in healthcare education and leadership by providing a seamless and personalised experience for all stakeholders. **Progress to date*** Programme governance structure in place * Programme and project teams in place * Organisation design structure updated * As‑Is and To‑Be mapping complete * Business requirements defined and documented * Some readiness projects complete * Procurement partner selection complete **Current Status*** Procurement activities commencing * Discovery and validation phase commencing * Implementation readiness projects underway #### **Job Description** **Role:** Test Analyst **Duration:** 2\-year fixed\-term contract **Salary:** 47,000 \+ Benefits **Closing Date:** 1 May 2026 at 16:00 The Test Analyst will support the successful delivery of the RCPI Connect Programme by designing, executing, and reporting on testing activities to ensure that new and enhanced digital services meet business requirements, quality standards, and regulatory obligations. Working as part of the Connect testing team, the Test Analyst will collaborate closely with programme workstreams, business stakeholders, IT teams, and external vendors to verify that solutions are fit for purpose, reliable, and ready for operational use. The role reports to the Test Manager and works alongside Test Leads and other Test Analysts across the Connect Programme, partnering with Business Analysts, developers, DevOps, and external vendors. **Key Deliverables (first 3–6 months):** * Traceability in place between requirements/acceptance criteria and test cases. * Test cases and regression pack drafted/updated for allocated workstreams. * Functional and integration test execution evidence captured and available for audit. * Defects logged with clear reproduction steps, prioritised via triage, and re\-tested to closure. * UAT support pack prepared (guidance, scripts, and defect support) and UAT outcomes captured. * Regular status updates provided to the Test Manager/Test Lead, including risks and blockers. **Key Responsibilities:** **Test Design \& Preparation*** Analyse business and functional requirements to ensure they are clear, testable, and aligned to intended outcomes. * Design and maintain test cases and test scripts that are traceable back to requirements and acceptance criteria. * Work with the Test Manager and Test Leads to identify testing risks, issues, and dependencies. * Contribute to test approach discussions (e.g., agile/hybrid delivery, shift\-left testing) and ensure test coverage aligns to user stories, acceptance criteria, and planned releases. * Coordinate test data and environment needs with IT/DevOps and vendors (e.g., access, refreshes, data masking), escalating constraints and risks as needed. **Test Execution*** Execute functional (including regression, integration, and system tests) as well as non\-functional where required in line with agreed test plans. * Ensure regression testing is carried out in a risk\-based manner to minimise the introduction of defects. * Support User Acceptance Testing (UAT) activities, working with business users to validate outcomes and resolve issues. * Execute agreed non\-functional checks where required (e.g., accessibility, performance, security, auditability, and data integrity) and support remediation validation. **Defect Management \& Reporting*** Log, track, and manage defects through to resolution using agreed tooling (e.g. Jira or equivalent). * Participate in defect triage discussions with internal teams and external vendors. * Contribute to test progress reporting and test metrics, including execution status and defect trends. **Collaboration \& Continuous Improvement*** Work closely with Connect workstreams, business units, and delivery partners to understand service impacts and delivery priorities. * Share knowledge, templates, and good practices with peers, and support onboarding of new test resources where applicable. * Contribute to the continuous improvement of testing practices, standards, and documentation across the Connect Programme. #### **Requirements** **Essential Experience \& Skills** * 2–3 years’ hands\-on software testing experience, ideally within multi\-phase or complex delivery programmes. * Experience reviewing requirements, specifications, and design documentation to provide meaningful testing input. * Strong test design skills, with experience writing clear, concise, and comprehensive test cases using a test management tool. * Experience executing functional, regression, and integration tests. * Experience managing defects through to resolution using a defect management tool. * Strong attention to detail and a clear commitment to quality. * Ability to plan, prioritise, and report on daily testing activities. * Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to work collaboratively across multiple teams. **Desirable Experience*** Experience with common QA tooling (e.g., Jira with Xray/Zephyr, Azure DevOps, TestRail, or similar). * Experience in testing data migrations or complex system integrations. * Experience testing enterprise platforms (e.g. CRM, ERP, or integrated digital systems). * Experience working in regulated environments, such as healthcare, education, or professional bodies. * Familiarity with GDPR and data protection considerations in system delivery. **Qualifications*** Degree\-level qualification (NFQ Level 7 or equivalent) in Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related discipline, or equivalent professional experience. * Relevant testing or quality assurance certification desirable but not essential. This is a unique opportunity to contribute to the testing and quality assurance activities of a high\-impact transformation that will shape the future of RCPI. As a Test Analyst on the Connect Programme, you will play an important role in ensuring that new digital services are delivered safely, reliably, and to the highest quality standards. **Competencies:** **1\. Test Analysis \& Design** Translates business and system requirements into clear, structured test scenarios and test cases with appropriate coverage. **2\.** **Test Execution \& Defect Management** Executes tests accurately, records results, and logs, retests, and supports resolution of defects in line with agreed processes. **3\. Quality \& Attention to Detail** Applies a strong quality mindset to identify defects, data issues, and edge cases, ensuring outcomes meet acceptance criteria. 4\. **Collaboration \& Communication** Works effectively with Test Managers, Business Analysts, developers, and business users, clearly communicating progress, risks, and issues. If you are passionate about quality, collaboration, and enabling meaningful outcomes through robust testing and assurance, we would welcome your application. Please submit your CV and a cover letter outlining your software testing experience and your ability to work collaboratively in complex delivery environments.#### **Why us?** **Leave:** We offer 21 days annual leave with an additional 3\-4 college days as well as long service leave to all employees. We also provide support for staff with Maternity, Sick, Parents, and Parental leave. **Wellbeing:** An Employee Assistance Programme with Spectrum Life is available to all staff members as well as annual flu vaccine. **Flexible Hybrid working model:** The RCPI offer a flexible 35\-hour\-a\-week hybrid working model for all employees from their start date, giving staff the autonomy to work from home combined with time spent onsite. **Pension:** A defined contribution pension scheme of 1\.5% Employee contribution and RCPI contribution from commencement date with an increase after 18 months **Flexible Benefit:** Pro\-rata €1,500 annual flexible benefit upon completion of a successful probationary period. **Life Assurance:** 4 times your annual salary. **Learning \& Development:** We offer CPD options including study leave and unlimited access to LinkedIn Learning. **Diversity and Inclusion**: RCPI recognises the importance of equality, diversity, and inclusion. We were recently awarded a Silver accreditation from Investors in Diversity. We are an open and inclusive organisation that celebrates and welcomes diversity. We lead initiatives that support, promote and demonstrate a culture of mutual respect, inclusivity, and diversity, as outlined in our Diversity and Inclusion policy. #### **Accommodations** The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland is an inclusive employer and in line with our policies you are invited to disclose any reasonable accommodations you require for applying, interviewing or working with RCPI. Please be assured this information will be treated as confidential and not used for the purpose of selection. If you have any queries, please contact Suzann Donnelly at suzanndonnelly@rcpi.ie. #### **About us** The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland is a postgraduate medical training college dedicated to ensuring that doctors have the skills to provide patients with the best possible care. Founded in 1654, the college supports doctors to enhance their skills, competencies, and professionalism throughout their working lives. Through its Faculties and Institutes, The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, offers medical training programmes, examinations, diplomas, and educational courses to equip doctors with the skills and knowledge they need to care for patients and run safe and efficient healthcare systems. It also offers a range of educational diplomas and courses for learners in the wider healthcare sector.

title: Treasury Analyst - Dublin
description: **Country:** Ireland **City:** Dublin **Req ID:** 522998 **Job Type**: Full Time **Workplace Type**: Onsite **Seniority Level**: Associate **About CRH** CRH is the leading provider of building materials critical to modernizing infrastructure. with out team of 83,000 people across 4,000 locations, our unmatched scale, connected portfolio, and deep local relationships make us the partner of choice for transportation, water, and reindustrialization projects, shaping communities for a better tomorrow. CRH (NYSE: CRH) is a member of the S\&P 500 Index. For more information, visit www.crh.com. Without you noticing our products, we are everywhere you live, work, and relax. Our project portfolio includes some of the most sustainable and cutting\-edge building projects around the world. Think of the asphalt on the Silverstone Grand Prix Circuit, the Paris Metro Rail project, but also the Louis Vuitton Museum in Paris, parts of the Burj Khalifa, and the Kennedy Space Centre. CRH Treasury is continuing to grow and expand. A vacancy has arisen within the Treasury Operations team for a Corporate Treasury Analyst to join this highly successful team. The team is based in the Group’s head office in Stonemason’s Way, Rathfarnham, Dublin 16\. Due to the nature of this role the successful candidate will be required on\-site initially for training and thereafter working arrangements under our group hybrid policy. **Key Tasks and Responsibilities:** =================================== The successful candidate will become a valuable member of the existing treasury operations/dealing team where the tasks and responsibilities below will be performed as part of the treasury operations team: * Take a leading role on Liquidity management across multi\-currency cash pools within the group. * Manage the Deposit book, commercial paper and other investment products and execution of the same. * Foreign exchange and commodity management and execution of hedges using derivative products: * Counterparty risk management monitoring and reporting. * Performing controls to monitor and report on the daily Treasury activities and processes. * Manage the process in conjunction with the Strategic projects team on Administration of internal funding arrangements. * Responsibility for the Treasury payments processing through the companies’ payments systems and banking portals. * Bank cash balance monitoring and reporting of cash held throughout the group (MT 940\). * Cash management and other banking duties. * Reporting daily and on an ad hoc basis as required on Treasury activities and processes. * Assisting and training junior members in the production of monthly KPI reporting to senior management. * Supporting various projects and initiatives in treasury operations (design, implementation and identification of process improvements). * Supporting the team through various SOX requirements, systems audits and internal audits throughout the year. **Education and Experience:** ============================= * University degree in accounting/finance or professional accounting / treasury qualification (preferred) * A minimum of 3\+ years post qualification experience. * Must be professionally minded, motivated and a self\-starter keen to make a strong contribution to the Treasury team as a function within the Treasury department. * Ability to prioritise your tasks as part of a team plan and work to deadlines on a daily basis. * Can learn and apply controls and processes within tasks and systems. * Can understand complex situations and processes and explain them simply to senior management. * Work with people across internal functions, external banking relationships and Treasury system providers. * Sees opportunities and appreciates a drive for continuous improvement and best practices. This is an ideal opportunity to join a fast\-moving and dynamic function within the CRH Finance department. **What CRH Offers You** * A culture that values opportunity for growth, development, and internal promotion * Highly competitive salary package * Comprehensive secondary benefits * Significant contribution to your pension plan * Health and wellness programs, including an on\-site gym and fitness classes * Excellent opportunities to develop and progress with a global organization **Connect your future to CRH** We are curious to learn more about you. At CRH, we believe our mutual differences contribute to the healthy, productive, and enjoyable workspace we create. Please introduce yourself and send us your application. Is this role not for you, but do you know someone who would love to join the team? Please let us know! CRH finds it important that vacancies are shared to individuals that may find them interesting and/or could be suitable for the role Please contact our recruitment team at careers@crh.com. *CRH is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to creating an inclusive work environment for all employees and actively encourage applications from all sectors of the community.* *Benefits/perks listed above may vary depending on the nature of the employment with CRH and the country where you work.* ***Please note that we cannot accept any applications submitted through email for GDPR purposes. Candidates must apply through our job portal.*** ***We do not accept candidate introductions for this position from recruitment agencies, unless you have been instructed to do so by our recruitment team.***

title: Trust & Safety Escalations Analyst
description: **Escalations Specialist \| 6\-Month Contract \| €60,000 Pro Rata \| 100% Onsite (Dublin)** We are currently hiring an **Escalations Specialist** to join a high\-performing global operations team supporting one of the world’s leading social media platforms. This is a **6\-month contract role** , offering a salary of **€60,000 per annum (pro rata)** and is **fully onsite in Dublin** . **The Role** As an Escalations Specialist, you will be responsible for managing and resolving complex, high\-priority cases across a global platform. You will work at the intersection of **policy, operations, and risk** , handling sensitive escalations that require strong judgment and analytical thinking. **Key Responsibilities** * Review, investigate, and resolve complex escalations across social media platforms * Interpret and enforce platform policies, including Community Standards, Advertising Policies, and Intellectual Property guidelines * Handle sensitive cases involving legal, press, and high\-profile user escalations * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams including Legal, Public Policy, Product, and Operations * Analyse escalation trends and provide recommendations to improve processes and policy enforcement * Communicate clearly with internal and external stakeholders on high\-impact issues * Work with potentially sensitive or objectionable content as part of investigations **What We’re Looking For** * 5–7 years’ experience in operations, trust \& safety, escalations, consulting, or a similar environment * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills * Experience managing high\-priority or complex cases end\-to\-end * Ability to work under pressure and navigate ambiguous or grey\-area scenarios * Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills * Highly organised and proactive approach **Nice to Have** * Experience working with policy enforcement or trust \& safety environments * Familiarity with tools such as Excel or SQL for data analysis * Understanding of the social media landscape and evolving online risks * Degree in Business, Legal, Public Policy, or a related field If you’re interested in working in a fast\-paced, high\-impact environment where decisions matter, apply now or reach out directly for more information

title: Trust & Safety New Associate - Hatespeech - Czech (CL13)
description: **Position:** Trust \& Safety New Associate \- Czech \- CL13 **Language:** Czech **Location:** Dublin, Ireland \- Eastpoint D3 **Contract:** 12 months contract (with view to extension) **Work Hours:** 24/7 What are we looking for: Primary skill \- Content Moderation \- P5 . Designation: Trust \& Safety New Associate. Roles and Responsibilities: In this role you are required to solve routine problems, largely through precedent and referral to general guidelines. Your primary interaction is within your own team and your direct supervisor. In this role you will be given detailed instructions on all tasks. title: Utilities Engineer
description: We are currently working with a leading organisation in a regulated manufacturing environment to hire a Plant Engineer for their Dublin site. This is a key hire within the engineering team, where you will play a lead role supporting the Maintenance Manager while taking ownership of site\-wide equipment across process, utilities, and tank farm areas. The position offers strong technical exposure along with the opportunity to drive reliability, improvements, and engineering standards across the site. **The Role:** * Act as the technical expert for your area, ensuring optimal performance of process and utility equipment * Lead maintenance activities across pumps, motors, HVAC, PLC\-controlled systems and instrumentation * Diagnose and resolve mechanical, electrical and automation faults, minimising downtime * Manage and coordinate service providers and contractors on\-site * Support and plan electrical shutdowns in line with safety and operational requirements * Lead root cause analysis on equipment failures and implement corrective actions * Drive continuous improvement initiatives across maintenance and reliability * Ensure full compliance with EHS and GMP standards, including accurate documentation **What We Are Looking For:** * 3\+ years’ experience in a plant/equipment engineering role within a regulated environment * Strong exposure to utilities and process systems such as HVAC, purified water, clean steam or refrigeration * Hands\-on experience troubleshooting across mechanical, electrical and automation systems * Experience working in a GMP environment with strong compliance awareness * Engineering qualification (degree or equivalent experience)
